view.s475.ben.s199a_nope: + § 199A QBI — generally NOT eligible (specified service trade — investment management)
view.s475.ben.s212_above_line: + Above-line Schedule C deductions (no § 67(g) 2% AGI floor)
view.s475.ben.s481_a_payment: - § 481(a) catch-up adjustment may be large + payable in year of change
view.s475.ben.unrealized_loss: - Year-end MTM forces unrealized loss recognition (cash flow + tax)
view.s475.elect.attached: Statement attached to timely filed return for PRIOR year

view.s475.h1.title: // § 475 TRADER MARK-TO-MARKET ELECTION
view.s475.hint.intro: § 475(f) qualified trader MTM election: gain/loss ordinary + NO § 1091 wash sale + Schedule C above-line expenses. Election due April 15 unextended.
view.s475.id.brokerage: Multiple brokerage accounts can serve as natural separation
view.s475.id.late_id: Late identification → security MTM applied; subsequent sale = ordinary
view.s475.id.no_id: No identification: all securities MTM, ordinary
view.s475.id.purpose: Permits dealer/trader to hold INVESTMENT securities separately
view.s475.id.same_day: Identification ON SAME DAY of acquisition (Reg § 1.475(c)-2)
view.s475.id.separate_account: Best practice: separate "investment account" (NOT MTM) from "trading account" (MTM)
view.s475.qt.daily_swings: Profit motivation: daily price swings, NOT long-term appreciation + dividends
view.s475.qt.endicott_factors: Endicott v. Comm. 145 T.C. 11 (2015): 4 factors weight
view.s475.qt.facts_circumstances: Facts & circumstances test — NO bright lines

view.s475.qt.partnership_trader: Partnership trader: each partner's election may differ

view.s475.qt.short_holding: Short avg holding period (days to weeks, NOT months)

view.s48.bonus.allocation_required: LMI bonus requires PRE-APPROVAL via Treasury / DOE Capacity Allocation Program
view.s48.bonus.base: BASE rate: 6% (without wage / apprentice OR ≥ 1 MW)
view.s48.bonus.domestic: +10% Domestic Content: ≥ 40% steel + iron domestic; manuf'd products % varies
view.s48.bonus.energy: +10% Energy Community: brownfield / fossil-fuel community / unemployment census
view.s48.bonus.full: FULL rate: 30% (with wage + apprentice OR under 1 MW)
view.s48.bonus.lmi: +10-20% LMI bonus: low-income census tract or affordable housing — competitive allocation
view.s48.bonus.under_1mw: < 1 MW projects: full 30% rate WITHOUT wage / apprentice compliance required
view.s48.h1.title: // § 48 INVESTMENT TAX CREDIT
view.s48.hint.intro: 30% credit on qualified energy property basis (solar, geothermal, fuel cell, wind ≤ 100kW, biogas, CHP, micro-turbine, etc.). 5× bonus structure: base 6% + 24% prevailing wage / apprenticeship multiplier. Adders: +10% domestic content + 10% energy community + 10% LMI bonus (low-income community). Post-2024: § 48E 'Clean Electricity ITC' replaces. § 6417 direct pay tax-exempt; § 6418 transferability taxable. Forms 3468 + 3800.
view.s48.rec.basis_reduce: Basis reduction: 50% of credit (separate basis reduction for adders)
view.s48.rec.casualty_exception: Casualty replacement: no recapture if rebuilt within rules
view.s48.rec.exit_planning: Hold 5+ yrs to avoid recapture — common in tax equity partnerships
view.s48.rec.recapture_form: Form 4255 to compute recapture if triggered
view.s48.rec.transferability: Transferability: recapture stays w/ ORIGINAL TAXPAYER (seller); buyer protected
view.s48.rec.triggers: Triggers: sale, abandonment, theft, casualty, lease change, change of use
view.s48.s48e.45y_ptc: § 45Y is PTC counterpart (operating credit per kWh)
view.s48.s48e.choice: Choose § 48 (legacy) OR § 48E (post-2024) — projects in service 2025+ default to § 48E
view.s48.s48e.future: Long-term: extends until US grid hits 25% of 2022 emissions
view.s48.s48e.phase_in: Begins 2025; § 48 phases out for new projects
view.s48.s48e.phaseout: Phase-out: 100% credit until 2032; phases out as GHG goals met
view.s48.s48e.tech_neutral: Technology-neutral: any zero-emission electricity generation qualifies
view.s481.cc.dcn123: DCN 123: § 461(h) economic performance recurring item exception
view.s481.cc.dcn235: DCN 235: De minimis tangible property election (§ 263(a))
view.s481.cc.dcn7_overall: DCN 7 overall method change automatic if < $25M gross receipts
view.s481.ex.cash_accrual: Cash → Accrual (small business hit $30M GR threshold)
view.s481.ex.completed_contract: Completed contract → percentage of completion (long-term contracts)
view.s481.ex.depreciation: Depreciation method (incl bonus depreciation election out)
view.s481.ex.inventory: Inventory: FIFO vs LIFO vs average vs lower of cost or market
view.s481.h1.title: // § 481(a) ACCOUNTING METHOD CHANGE
view.s481.hint.intro: Form 3115. § 481(a) adjustment captures difference between old + new method. Positive (increases income): spread over 4 yrs OR year of change for de minimis < $50k. Negative (decreases income): fully in year of change. Automatic (Rev. Proc. 2024-14): no IRS consent, file with return. Non-automatic: advance consent, $11,500 user fee, 6-month review.
view.s481.proc.attach: Attach Form 3115 to first year's return (signed schedule + supporting computation)
view.s481.proc.audit_consequences: If IRS-audit-initiated: may treat as taxpayer-initiated under Rev. Proc. 2002-18
view.s481.proc.automatic: Automatic: Form 3115 with return, no IRS consent, no user fee, late filing relief available
view.s481.proc.consistency: Must use new method consistently going forward
view.s481.proc.duplicate: Send duplicate copy to IRS National Office Ogden, UT (since 2024)
view.s481.proc.identify: Identify if method change needed (Rev. Proc. 2022-14 etc. lists automatic changes)
view.s481.proc.non_automatic: Non-automatic: $11,500 user fee + advance consent (6 mo review)
view.s481.proc.notice_2022_36: Notice 2022-36 disaster relief: timely filing extension
view.s481.proc.s481_d: § 481(d) recovery of items omitted under prior method - special rules
view.s481.proc.signature: Officer / partner / sole owner signs Form 3115
view.s481.proc.timing: File Form 3115 with timely-filed return (incl. extensions) for year of change
view.s481.proc.under_examination: Under audit / examination: Form 3115 generally NOT permitted (limited exceptions)
view.s481.rel.s6662: § 6662 accuracy-related penalty — may apply if § 481 not properly reported
view.s481.rel.s9100: § 9100 relief — automatic 12-month extension for late Form 3115
view.s481.s446.consent: § 446(e) requires consent of Secretary for method change
view.s481.s446.correction_of_error: Correction of error in prior method NOT subject to consent (revocation possible)
view.s481.s446.first_year: First year using method: NOT a method change (no § 481(a))
view.s481.s446.s481_aceflyer: Schedule M-3 / M-1 reconciliation may reveal book/tax difference triggering change
view.s481.s446.s481_safe: Compliance: filing Form 3115 = "filed return" with method
view.s481.s446.short_form: Short Form 3115: certain automatic changes (less detailed)
view.s481.spread.audit_initiated: IRS-initiated change: typically 1-year for unfavorable (no taxpayer relief)
view.s481.spread.cessation: If business ceases / no longer using method: remaining § 481(a) accelerated
view.s481.spread.favorable: FAVORABLE (decrease income): 1-year (full in year of change)
view.s481.spread.s481_a_post_tcja: Post-TCJA: § 451(b) AFS inclusion conformity — major mass change
view.s481.spread.s481_b: § 481(b) relief: 3-yr average tax computation limits tax increase
view.s481.spread.s481_b_election: Election: tax = 3-yr avg × 3 OR 1-yr × current rate — taxpayer chooses lower
view.s481.spread.small_dollar: $50K or less unfavorable: ANNUAL election for 1-year (taxpayer's choice)
view.s482.apa.body: Multi-year prospective agreement with IRS on transfer pricing methodology. Three types: Unilateral (IRS only), Bilateral (US + 1 foreign country), Multilateral (US + multiple). 5-year term typical, renewable. Strong protection from § 6662 penalties + foreign disputes (competent authority). User fee $113,500 (small business reduced).
view.s482.doc.background: Background documents: contracts, transfer-pricing studies, agreements
view.s482.doc.economic: Economic analysis: method selection + comparable selection
view.s482.doc.org_chart: Organization chart + comp data + related party identification
view.s482.doc.overview: Overview of business: organizational structure, ownership, operations, market
view.s482.h1.title: // § 482 TRANSFER PRICING
view.s482.hint.intro: IRS may allocate income / deductions among related parties to clearly reflect income. Standards: arm's-length transactions, comparability + best method analysis. Methods: CUP, RPM, CPM, PSM, TNMM. § 6662(e) penalty: 20% substantial / 40% gross misstatement if no contemporaneous documentation. Six principal documents required for protection. APA (Advance Pricing Agreement) provides certainty.
view.s483.afr.applicable_rate: 'Applicable rate' used to test adequate interest
view.s483.afr.long: Long-term AFR: applies to debt > 9 years (long bond yields)
view.s483.afr.mid: Mid-term AFR: applies to debt > 3 to ≤ 9 years (3-9 year Treasury)
view.s483.afr.published: Published monthly in Rev. Rul. (e.g., Rev. Rul. 2024-X)
view.s483.afr.short: Short-term AFR: applies to debt ≤ 3 years (90-day Treasury)
view.s483.coord.estate: Estate planning: low-AFR sale to grantor trust (private annuity sale)
view.s483.coord.s1239: § 1239 related party: combine with imputed interest analysis
view.s483.coord.s1274: § 1274 debt instruments: applies to PUBLICLY TRADED debt (not § 483)
view.s483.coord.s2032a: § 2032A estate freezes use installment notes with AFR
view.s483.coord.s453: § 453 installment method: imputed interest part NOT subject to installment
view.s483.coord.s453_b_g: § 453B(g): transferred installment notes to spouse — tax-free under § 1041
view.s483.coord.s7702: § 7702 life insurance contracts: separate test, not § 483
view.s483.coord.s7872: § 7872 below-market loans: similar imputation for non-sale loans
view.s483.exc.cash_method: Cash method seller + small amounts: may opt out
view.s483.exc.de_minimis: De minimis: aggregate ≤ $10K sales of property in a year
view.s483.exc.publicly_traded: Publicly traded property: § 1274 may apply instead
view.s483.exc.small_personal: Small personal use property: ≤ $250K sales (e.g., cars)
view.s483.h1.title: // § 483 IMPUTED INTEREST
view.s483.hint.intro: Installment sales with inadequate interest: portion of each payment recharacterized as INTEREST. Test rate: AFR (Applicable Federal Rate) based on term + compounding period. Below-AFR loans: portion of each payment = interest (not capital gain). § 7872 below-market loans + § 1274 debt instruments coordinate. 3 AFR rates: short-term (≤ 3 yrs), mid-term (3-9 yrs), long-term (> 9 yrs). De minimis: ≤ $10K sales without imputed interest.
view.s483.imp.allocation: Allocate to interest first, then to principal recovery
view.s483.imp.below: If stated rate < AFR: portion of each payment = imputed interest
view.s483.imp.buyer_basis: Buyer's basis = principal portion (NOT total payments)
view.s483.imp.character: Interest portion: ORDINARY INCOME for seller, deductible (§ 163) for buyer
view.s483.imp.formula: Imputed interest = AFR × outstanding balance per period
view.s483.imp.principal: Principal portion: capital gain (per § 453 installment method)
view.s483.imp.section_1274: § 1274 may apply instead for 'publicly traded' debt instruments
view.s483.imp.test: Test: stated rate ≥ AFR → adequate interest; no imputation
view.s483.related_note: BELOW-AFR + RELATED PARTY: § 483 imputed interest combined w/ § 7872 below-market loan rules + § 1239 related party rules. IRS scrutiny on private installment sales among family. Document at-least-AFR stated rate. Estate planning structure (intra-family sales) requires careful AFR documentation + annual interest reporting.
view.s48C.h1.title: // § 48C ADVANCED ENERGY
view.s48C.hint.intro: Competitive allocation by DOE: 30% ITC (with wage + apprenticeship) on energy property + advanced manufacturing equipment. Reauthorized IRA 2022 with $10B (40% reserved for energy communities). Round 1: $4B (Mar 2023); Round 2: $6B (Jan 2024). Reserved for: clean energy manufacturing + critical minerals + industrial decarbonization. 2-yr placed-in-service after allocation. Forms 3468 + 3800 + DOE concept paper + full app. § 6418 transferability allowed; NO § 6417 direct pay for § 48C.
view.s48C.no_alloc_note: NO § 48C credit without DOE allocation. Apply at energy.gov 48C-PORTAL: 8-page concept paper → encouragement letter → 30-page full application → allocation. Process takes 6-9 months. Consider § 48 ITC (automatic, no allocation) as alternative.
view.s48C.proc.allocation: Step 4: DOE selects + Treasury allocates credit amount
view.s48C.proc.encourage: Step 2: DOE 'Encouragement Letter' — proceed with full application
view.s48C.proc.evaluation_criteria: Evaluation: (1) commercial viability, (2) job creation, (3) GHG reduction, (4) supply chain diversification
view.s48C.proc.full_app: Step 3: 30-page full application + technical details + commercial plan
view.s48C.proc.placed: Step 5: Place in service within 2 years of allocation (extension up to 1 yr)
view.s48C.proc.recapture: Step 7: 5-yr recapture period (sale, abandonment, change of use)
view.s48C.round.beats_45x: Stacking: § 48C + § 45X possible if separate qualifying expenditures (different basis)
view.s48C.round.r1_amount: Round 1: $4B allocated (Mar 2023); 80 projects selected from 250+ applicants
view.s48C.round.r2_energy: Round 2: $2.5B for energy communities + 80% domestic content bonus
view.s48C.round.r3_pending: Round 3: pending congressional authorization for additional funding
view.s48C.round.success_rate: Award rate: ~30-40% of full applicants — competitive
view.s48C.round.timing: Timing: concept papers due ~Q1; full applications ~Q3; allocations ~Q4-Q1 next yr
view.s4940.ded.necessary: Other ordinary + necessary expenses related to income production
view.s4940.ded.no_charitable: NOT charitable program expenses (those go in Part I-A)
view.s4940.ded.tax: State + local property taxes on investment real estate
view.s4940.est.body: If § 4940 tax expected to exceed $500, quarterly estimated payments required by 5/15, 6/15, 9/15, 12/15. Form 990-W for computation. Safe harbor: 100% prior year OR 90% current year. Underpayment penalty under § 6655.
view.s4940.h1.title: // § 4940 PF NET INVESTMENT INCOME
view.s4940.hint.intro: 1.39% flat tax on PF net investment income (interest, dividends, royalties, rents, net capital gains). Pre-2020 was 2% with reduced 1% rate available if distribution test met — SECURE Act eliminated 2-tier, made flat 1.39%. Reported on Form 990-PF Part XI. Net long-term capital losses do NOT offset other income (PF specific). Quarterly estimated tax payments required if > $500.
view.s4940.inc.cap_gain: Net long-term capital gains (cap losses do NOT offset NII)
view.s4940.inc.short_term: Short-term capital gains (treated as ordinary income)
view.s4941.dq.35_owned: Entity 35%+ owned by combination of disqualified persons
view.s4941.dq.family: Family member (spouse, ancestor, descendant + their spouses)
view.s4941.dq.substantial: Substantial contributor (≥ $5k AND > 2% of total contributions historically)
view.s4941.ex.bank_general: Bank deposit accounts at general commercial terms
view.s4941.ex.incidental: Incidental benefit from charitable activities (e.g., banquet seat)
view.s4941.ex.no_charge: Free use of property by foundation
view.s4941.ex.no_interest_loan: Interest-free / no-interest loans TO foundation (from DP)
view.s4941.ex.reasonable_comp: Reasonable comp for personal services NECESSARY to foundation
view.s4941.h1.title: // § 4941 PF SELF-DEALING
view.s4941.hint.intro: Private foundation can't transact with 'disqualified persons' (substantial contributor, manager, family, 35% owned entity). STRICT LIABILITY — no intent required. 10% on disqualified person + 5% on knowing manager INITIAL. 200% on DP + 50% on manager if not corrected within taxable period. Even 'fair price' transactions PROHIBITED. Form 4720 + Form 990-PF.
view.s4941.rel.4945: § 4945: 20% on taxable expenditures (lobbying, political, individual grants)
view.s4942.cf.fifo: FIFO usage: oldest carryover absorbed first
view.s4942.cf.set_aside: Set-aside elections require IRS approval + 5-yr limit
view.s4942.cf.transition: Newly-converted PF: 4-yr transition rule for full payout requirement
view.s4942.cf.unusual_grant: Unusual grant exception (Rev. Proc. 76-13) for one-time mega contributions
view.s4942.h1.title: // § 4942 PF UNDISTRIBUTED INCOME
view.s4942.hint.intro: PF must distribute 5% of average non-charitable-use asset value annually for charitable purposes. 30% initial excise on undistributed + 100% if not corrected within taxable period. Carryforward 5 yrs. Form 990-PF Part XII. Operating foundations exempt (substantially-all assets directly used in charitable activities).
view.s4942.qual.amounts_paid: Amounts paid OR set aside for charitable purposes
view.s4942.qual.daf_no: Grants to DAFs DO count (since SECURE 2.0)
view.s4942.qual.direct: Direct charitable activities (educational programs, research, museum operations)
view.s4942.qual.exempt_purpose: Acquisition of property for exempt purposes
view.s4942.qual.foreign: Grants to foreign organizations with equivalency determination
view.s4942.qual.grants: Grants to public charities + foreign organizations (with expenditure responsibility)
view.s4942.qual.set_aside: Set-asides for specific future projects (5-yr limit + IRS approval)
view.s4943.disp.pri_conversion: Re-characterize as PRI through governance change (Newman 2022)
view.s4943.disp.sell_market: Sell to non-DP in arm's-length market transaction
view.s4943.disp.spin_off: Spin off the excess holdings to operating subsidiary
view.s4943.ex.de_minimis: De minimis: PF alone ≤ 2% always allowed regardless of aggregate
view.s4943.ex.passive_income: Passive income business (rents, royalties, etc.) — different rules
view.s4943.ex.secure_2_0: SECURE 2.0 Newman exception: solely-owned biz qualifies as PRI if all profits charity
view.s4943.ex.unrelated_business: Unrelated trade or business: not exempt; subject to UBTI
view.s4943.h1.title: // § 4943 PF EXCESS BUSINESS HOLDINGS
view.s4943.hint.intro: PF + disqualified persons may hold up to 20% of voting stock (35% if 3rd-party effective control). De minimis: PF alone ≤ 2% always permitted. 5-yr divestiture period for gifts / bequests (extendable). 10% initial excise on excess + 200% if not corrected within taxable period. SECURE 2.0: 100% solely-owned biz holdings can qualify as Program-Related Investment (PRI).
view.s4944.h1.title: // § 4944 PF JEOPARDIZING INVESTMENTS
view.s4944.hint.intro: PF managers must exercise ordinary business care + prudence — investments jeopardizing charitable purpose = excise. 10% initial on PF + 10% on manager (knowing). 25% PF + 5% additional manager if not corrected. § 4944(c) PRI EXEMPT — primary purpose charitable + no significant income purpose + no lobbying / political. Most foundations use modern portfolio theory.
view.s4944.pri.counts_payout: PRIs COUNT toward § 4942 5% minimum payout requirement
view.s4944.pri.examples: Examples: micro-loans, low-interest community development loans, charter school bonds
view.s4944.pri.no_income: No SIGNIFICANT purpose to produce income
view.s4944.pri.no_political: No purpose to influence legislation / political campaigns
view.s4944.pri.primary: Primary purpose: accomplish exempt purposes (charitable / educational / scientific)
view.s4944.pri.terms_loans: Below-market loans + equity in social enterprises common PRIs
view.s4944.prud.long_short: Long-term + short-term financial needs of PF
view.s4944.prud.no_per_se: NO per se prohibited investments under modern prudent investor rule
view.s4944.prud.review: Periodic portfolio review by qualified advisors
view.s4944.prud.uniform_act: Uniform Prudent Management of Institutional Funds Act (UPMIFA) — state law overlay
view.s4945.er.cost_segregation: Grantee separately accounts for grant funds
view.s4945.er.pre_grant: Pre-grant inquiry: ensure grantee + funds will be used per grant terms
view.s4945.er.records: PF retains records demonstrating proper use
view.s4945.er.recovery: Take appropriate steps if funds misused (refund / lawsuit)
view.s4945.er.report_to_irs: PF reports grants under expenditure responsibility on Form 990-PF Schedule O
view.s4945.er.written: Written grant agreement specifying charitable use
view.s4945.h1.title: // § 4945 PF TAXABLE EXPENDITURES
view.s4945.hint.intro: 20% initial PF + 5% manager (knowing) for: (1) lobbying / political, (2) grants to individuals without expenditure responsibility, (3) grants to non-public-charity orgs without expenditure responsibility, (4) non-charitable purpose. 100% PF + 50% manager if not corrected within taxable period. Manager penalty capped at $20,000 per expenditure.
view.s4945.indiv.advance: Pre-approval request to IRS prior to grant
view.s4945.indiv.alternative_er: Alternative: use expenditure responsibility instead of pre-approval
view.s4945.indiv.employer_relate: No relationship to PF / disqualified persons (anti-abuse)
view.s4945.indiv.purposes: For travel / study / similar charitable / scientific / educational purposes
view.s4945.indiv.qualified: Selected from qualified group sufficiently broad
view.s4945.indiv.report: Grantee reports per agreement; PF reviews
view.s4945.lob.ballot_measures: Ballot measure advocacy MAY be lobbying (state-specific)
view.s4945.lob.no_propaganda: No carrying on propaganda / influencing legislation
view.s4945.lob.public_charity: Grant to public charity for general support: not lobbying even if charity lobbies
view.s4945.lob.specific_project: Specific lobbying-purpose grants ARE taxable expenditures
view.s4945.lob.technical: Technical advice in response to written request from gov body OK
view.s4958.corr.interest: Interest at § 6621 underpayment rate from date of excess
view.s4958.corr.no_double_recovery: DP cannot deduct repayment under § 165 (loss) — adjusts compensation
view.s4958.corr.repay: DP repays excess to organization (with interest)
view.s4958.corr.s4958_d: § 4958(d) — extends correction period during deficiency review
view.s4958.corr.s4961_abatement: § 4961 — IRS may abate § 4958(b) 200% on timely correction
view.s4958.corr.timing: Within taxable period = ending 90 days after FIRST notice of deficiency
view.s4958.dp.501c3_excluded: EXCLUDED: § 501(c)(3) public charity (org itself), employees making < $130K (2024 indexed), volunteers
view.s4958.dp.daf_donor: DAF: donor + family members are DPs to fund
view.s4958.dp.factors: Factors: voting power, founding role, financial contributions, structure of authority
view.s4958.dp.family: FAMILY MEMBERS: spouse, ancestors, descendants, siblings + spouses
view.s4958.dp.former: FORMER DP for 5 years after substantial influence ends
view.s4958.dp.regs: Reg § 53.4958-3 factors test for substantial influence
view.s4958.dp.substantial: PERSON WITH SUBSTANTIAL INFLUENCE: officer, director, key employee, etc.
view.s4958.ebt.compensation: Compensation: salary + bonuses + perks + retirement + § 79 life insurance
view.s4958.ebt.economic_benefit: "Economic benefit" includes loans, rentals, sales (FMV vs paid)
view.s4958.ebt.formula: Excess benefit = VALUE PROVIDED TO DP minus CONSIDERATION DP gave to org
view.s4958.ebt.partial_release: Forgiveness of debt, payment of personal expenses
view.s4958.ebt.s4958_c_2: § 4958(c)(2) automatic excess benefit — DAF grants to DP — ENTIRE grant excess
view.s4958.ebt.scholarship: Scholarship to DP: typically excess benefit
view.s4958.ebt.timing: Timing: when economic benefit becomes ascertainable + non-refundable
view.s4958.h1.title: // § 4958 INTERMEDIATE SANCTIONS
view.s4958.hint.intro: 25%/200% excise on excess benefit + 10% OM penalty + § 4960 21% exec comp + rebuttable presumption (independent board + comparability + documentation).
view.s4958.rebut.benchmark: Benchmark data: 3+ similar orgs + similar geographic + sector
view.s4958.rebut.comparable: Comparability data: comparable orgs + arms-length salary surveys
view.s4958.rebut.contemporaneous: Contemporaneous documentation: minutes, comp committee, salary survey data

view.s4958.rebut.no_safe_harbor: Without safe harbor: facts & circumstances burden on DP/org
view.s4958.rebut.satisfaction: Satisfying all 3 → presumption of reasonableness (burden shifts to IRS)
view.s4958.s4960.deductibility: NOT deductible (no impact — org is exempt)
view.s4958.s4960.medical_exception: Medical services exception: doctors, vets, etc. — not counted
view.s4958.s4960.over_1m: Compensation > $1,000,000 to top-5 highest-paid → 21% on excess
view.s4958.s4960.parachute: Excess parachute payment: 3× base + 21% excise on excess
view.s4958.s4960.regs_2020: Final regs T.D. 9938 (2020): scope, computation, related party rules
view.s4958.s4960.scope: Applies to: § 501(a) exempt orgs, applicable § 521 cooperatives, etc.
view.s4958.s4960.tcja: TCJA-added: 21% excise on excess executive comp by tax-exempt orgs
view.s4958.s4960.top_5: Top 5: aggregated current + 1 prior year HCEs
view.s4960.cov.licensed_pros: Medical / veterinary licensed professionals: medical-services portion EXCLUDED
view.s4960.cov.notice_2019: Notice 2019-9 + Notice 2021-78 + final regs 2021 for guidance
view.s4960.cov.related_orgs: Related tax-exempt orgs aggregated for determination
view.s4960.cov.shareholders: Substantial shareholder of related taxable corp counted
view.s4960.excl.admin: NOT administrative / teaching / research at hospital
view.s4960.excl.allocation: Allocate dual-purpose comp pro-rata across medical vs other
view.s4960.h1.title: // § 4960 TAX-EXEMPT $1M COMP
view.s4960.hint.intro: 21% excise on tax-exempt EMPLOYER for: (a) Annual comp > $1M to covered employee, OR (b) 'Excess parachute payment' (≥ 3× base × disqualified) similar to § 280G but on tax-exempt side. Covered employee = top-5 highest-paid for any year since 2017 (sticky list — once covered, always covered). Form 4720. Universities, hospitals, foundations, large nonprofits primary targets.
view.s4960.label.name: Name / role
view.s4960.th.name: Name
view.s4972.coord.dc_db: DC + DB combined: § 404(a)(7) lesser of 25% comp OR aggregate deduction
view.s4972.coord.epcrs: EPCRS self-correction + voluntary correction + audit closing agreement
view.s4972.coord.s404: § 404(a) deduction limit (25% comp DC, actuarial DB) is the bright line
view.s4972.coord.s415: § 415(c) annual addition limit DIFFERENT from § 404 deduction limit
view.s4972.coord.s4979: § 4979 10% excise on excess elective deferrals (HCE refund)
view.s4972.cum.allocation: Multiple-employer plans: excise on employer making excess contribution
view.s4972.cum.annual: 10% applied EACH year excess remains undistributed
view.s4972.cum.compound: Compound effect: $100K excess for 3 years = $30K total excise
view.s4972.cum.deemed: Excess deemed reduced by current-year contribution shortfall
view.s4972.cum.distribution: Distribution of excess = withdrawal subject to income tax + § 72 early
view.s4972.cum.return: Trustee return of excess by Apr 15 next year avoids further excise
view.s4972.h1.title: // § 4972 NONDEDUCTIBLE PLAN CONTRIBUTION
view.s4972.hint.intro: 10% excise on nondeductible contributions to qualified plans. § 404 limit. Cumulative excise each year excess remains.
view.s4973.cause.foreign_excluded: Foreign earned income excluded (no qualifying income for IRA)
view.s4973.cause.hsa_partial: HSA partial year (eligibility changes mid-year + full contribution made)
view.s4973.cause.income_limit: Roth IRA income limit exceeded (high earner contributions)
view.s4973.cause.late_recharacterization: Late recharacterization (post-TCJA elimination of Roth → Traditional)
view.s4973.cause.spousal_earned: Spousal IRA without sufficient earned income
view.s4973.corr.absorb: Absorb in next year: count toward NEXT year's contribution (limited use)
view.s4973.corr.cumulative: Cumulative: 6% × YEAR after year on uncorrected excess
view.s4973.corr.deemed_distribution: Late correction: excess remains; future withdrawal = ordinary income
view.s4973.corr.form_5329: Form 5329: file with 1040 to report excess + pay excise
view.s4973.corr.formula: NIA = excess × (adj. earnings / total balance) — Reg § 1.408-11
view.s4973.corr.must_remove_earnings: MUST REMOVE ALSO: net income attributable to excess (NIA)
view.s4973.corr.recharacterize: Recharacterize traditional ↔ Roth: by Oct 15 (post-2017 TCJA limited)
view.s4973.corr.timely_withdraw: Timely withdrawal: by tax-filing deadline + extensions (typically Oct 15)
view.s4973.correct_now_note: CORRECTION WINDOW OPEN — days remaining. Contact IRA / HSA custodian to request 'Return of Excess Contribution' by tax-filing deadline (typically Oct 15). MUST also remove NIA (net income attributable). Avoid 6% excise tax permanently if completed timely. Form 5329 Part III to report.
view.s4973.h1.title: // § 4973 EXCESS IRA / HSA
view.s4973.hint.intro: 6% excise tax per year on excess contributions to IRAs, HSAs, Coverdell ESAs, Archer MSAs. CUMULATIVE: tax continues EACH year until excess removed + earnings withdrawn. Timely correction (by tax-filing deadline + extensions): avoids penalty + earnings withdrawn as ordinary income. Late correction: 6% excise on excess + earnings stay in account taxable later. Form 5329 Part III for IRAs, Part VII for HSAs, etc. Limit 2024: $7K IRA + $1K catch-up; $8,300 HSA family.
view.s4973.late_note: LATE CORRECTION: 6% excise tax accrues EACH YEAR until excess removed. Either: (1) take distribution now (taxable income) OR (2) absorb in future year's contribution limit if eligible. Note: distribution counts toward income limits + may trigger § 72(t) 10% if under age 59.5. Continued failure = 6% perpetual + cumulative.
view.s4973.lim.coverdell: Coverdell ESA: $2,000 per beneficiary (single $95-$110K income phase-out)
view.s4973.lim.ira_phase_roth: Roth IRA income limit: $146-$161K single / $230-$240K MFJ phase-out
view.s4973.lim.ira_trad: Traditional + Roth IRA: $7,000 (combined; age 50+ add $1,000 catch-up = $8,000)
view.s4973.lim.spousal: Spousal IRA: non-working spouse may contribute up to $7K if combined earned income
view.s4973.nia.10pct: Premature distribution under 59.5: 10% additional § 72(t) on NIA
view.s4973.nia.broker: Most brokers calculate NIA automatically when excess withdrawal requested
view.s4973.nia.cl_bal: Adj Cl Bal = Account balance AFTER excess + distributions removed
view.s4973.nia.daily: Daily NIA computation often required for trustee tracking
view.s4973.nia.formula: NIA = Excess × [(Adj Cl Bal - Adj Op Bal) / Adj Op Bal]
view.s4973.nia.negative: If NIA negative: loss on excess (basis adjustment only)
view.s4973.nia.op_bal: Adj Op Bal = Account balance BEFORE excess + other contributions during period
view.s4973.nia.positive: If NIA positive: earnings on excess included in income (year of distribution)
view.s4974.calc.aggregation_ira: Aggregation: multiple IRAs total RMD; satisfy from any one
view.s4974.calc.balance: Account balance: December 31 of PRIOR year
view.s4974.calc.beneficiary: Beneficiary RMDs: single life table (more aggressive)
view.s4974.calc.formula: RMD = Prior year-end balance / Distribution period divisor
view.s4974.calc.spousal_delay: Surviving spouse: may delay until spouse's own RBD
view.s4974.corr.10pct: SECURE 2.0: 10% (reduced from 25%) if corrected within 2-year window
view.s4974.corr.administrative: IRS administrative relief: typically waived if cured promptly + reasonable
view.s4974.corr.calculation: 2-year window: from year RMD shortfall first occurred
view.s4974.corr.illness: Reasonable cause examples: illness, financial advisor error, IRA custodian error
view.s4974.corr.late: Late even with waiver: penalty + amended return + Form 5329
view.s4974.corr.waiver: Reasonable cause waiver: still available; reasonable explanation + documentation
view.s4974.h1.title: // § 4974 RMD UNDERDISTRIBUTION
view.s4974.hint.intro: 25% excise tax (post-SECURE 2.0) on shortfall between Required Minimum Distribution (RMD) and actual. Reduced to 10% if corrected within 2-year window (post-SECURE 2.0). Apply to: 401(k), 403(b), 457(b), Traditional IRAs (NOT Roth while owner alive). RBD: April 1 of year after age 73 (SECURE 2.0, rising to 75 in 2033). First-year delay: can defer first RMD to April 1 of next year (but then take 2 in that year). Form 5329 Part IX reports.
view.s4974.qcd.coordination: Cannot send to donor-advised fund or supporting organization
view.s4974.qcd.no_double: Not taxable + does NOT count as itemized deduction (vs cash gift)
view.s4974.qcd.purpose: Direct IRA → 501(c)(3) charity transfer counts as RMD
view.s4974.qcd.reportable: Reported on 1099-R Box 1 + adjustment in Form 1040 Line 4b
view.s4974.qcd.tax_efficient: Tax-efficient: avoids inclusion in AGI (lowers Medicare premiums, SS taxation)
view.s4974.rbd.deferral_strategy: Deferral strategy: take 1st RMD by April 1, take 2nd in same year
view.s4974.rbd.employer_plans_delay: Employer plans: RBD = later of age 73 OR retirement (5%+ owners: age 73 regardless)
view.s4974.rbd.first_year: First-year delay: April 1 of year AFTER reaching RBD age
view.s4974.rbd.tax_implications: Both RMDs in same year may push income into higher tax bracket
view.s4974.shortfall_note: RMD SHORTFALL: 25% (or 10% if within 2-yr window) excise tax applies. Take missed distribution IMMEDIATELY + file Form 5329 Part IX. SECURE 2.0 introduced 2-year cure window for reduced 10% rate. Reasonable cause waiver still available — typically illness, error by IRA custodian / advisor. Avoid: setup automatic year-end RMD via brokerage.
view.s4975.dq.50_owned: Entity 50%+ owned by combination of above
view.s4975.dq.fiduciary: Plan fiduciary, anyone with discretion over IRA assets
view.s4975.dq.lineal: Ancestors + descendants (parents, kids, grandkids) + their spouses
view.s4975.dq.not_siblings: NOT siblings, NOT in-laws beyond spouse

view.s4975.ex.pte_84_14: PTE 84-14 (QPAM) — independent qualified professional asset manager
view.s4975.ex.section_408: § 408 ROBS (Rollover for Business Startups) C-corp structure — narrow
view.s4975.h1.title: // § 4975 PROHIBITED TRANSACTIONS
view.s4975.hint.intro: 15% excise on amount involved (per year) + 100% if uncorrected within taxable period. IRA PT = automatic distribution of ENTIRE IRA on Jan 1 of year of PT — § 408(e)(2). Most common traps in self-directed IRAs investing in real estate, private business, crypto. Disqualified persons: IRA holder, spouse, ancestors, descendants, fiduciaries (50% owned entities).
view.s4975.label.dist_amount: Total IRA balance at PT date ($)
view.s4975.trap.buy_from_self: Buying property already owned personally with IRA funds
view.s4975.trap.checkbook_warning: 'Checkbook IRA' / IRA-LLC: Swanson / Niemann ok IF structured right + no comingling
view.s4975.trap.commission: Receiving real estate commission on IRA's property purchase
view.s4975.trap.fix_it: Personally doing repairs on IRA-owned property (services to IRA)
view.s4975.trap.guarantee: Personally guaranteeing IRA loan (extension of credit)
view.s4975.trap.lend_to_co: Lending IRA funds to your closely-held biz
view.s4975.trap.live_in: Buying real estate IRA owns and living in it (use by you)
view.s4975.trap.rent_to_kid: Renting IRA-owned property to your child
view.s4980d.allowed.excepted: Excepted Benefit HRA: $2,100/yr cap, supplemental to group coverage
view.s4980d.allowed.fsa: Health FSA: limited annual carryover + 2.5-month grace period
view.s4980d.allowed.ichra: ICHRA: any employer, structured by class (full-time, part-time, etc.), requires individual mkt coverage
view.s4980d.allowed.integrated: Integrated HRA: pairs with employer group plan
view.s4980d.allowed.qsehra: QSEHRA: small employer (< 50 FTE), $5,850/$11,800 (2024) caps, reimburses individual market
view.s4980d.allowed.retiree: Retiree-only HRA: exempt from § 4980D when only covering ex-employees
view.s4980d.h1.title: // § 4980D HRA / GROUP HEALTH EXCISE
view.s4980d.hint.intro: $100/day per affected individual = up to $36,500/employee/year for ACA group market reform violations. Common: pre-2020 HRA paying individual-market premiums (Notice 2013-54), fee-for-service HRAs, ICHRAs without proper notices / classes. QSEHRA (small employer) safe under Cures Act. Annual report on Form 8928 if violation.
view.s4980d.relief.500k_cap: Annual cap: $500k or 10% of group plan aggregate health expenses
view.s4980d.relief.unintentional: Unintentional failure + first 30 days: no penalty
view.s4980h.fte.controlled: Controlled group / affiliated service group: aggregate FTEs across all entities
view.s4980h.fte.foreign: Foreign workers: excluded (no US source income)
view.s4980h.fte.measurement: Use look-back measurement (3-12 mo) + stability (6-12 mo) for variable hour
view.s4980h.h1.title: // § 4980H ACA EMPLOYER MANDATE
view.s4980h.hint.intro: Applicable Large Employer (ALE) = 50+ FTE in prior calendar year. (a) Penalty: $2,970 × ALL FTEs minus 30 if ANY employee gets PTC and you offer to < 95% of FTEs. (b) Penalty: $4,460 per non-covered FTE that gets PTC when offered NOT minimum value / NOT affordable. Affordability: employee share ≤ 8.39% of household income (W-2 safe harbor, FPL safe harbor available). Form 1095-C / 1094-C required for each employee + transmittal.
view.s4980h.sh.choose: Choose safest based on workforce composition
view.s4980h.sh.combine: Different safe harbor per 'reasonable category' of employees allowed
view.s4980h.sh.w2: W-2 safe harbor: lowest plan ≤ 8.39% of Box 1 W-2 wages
view.s51.grp.dcr: Designated community resident (16-39 in empowerment zone)
view.s51.grp.snap: SNAP recipient (food stamps for 3 of last 15 months)
view.s51.grp.tanf: TANF recipient (short-term 9 mo OR long-term 18 mo)
view.s51.grp.unemployed: Long-term unemployed (27+ consecutive weeks; verified Form 9061)
view.s51.h1.title: // § 51 WOTC
view.s51.hint.intro: Up to $2,400 per new hire from 9 targeted groups. $9,600 enhanced for qualified veterans with service-connected disability. 40% on first $6K wages (25% if < 400 hrs but ≥ 120); $0 if < 120 hrs. 50% on 2nd-year wages for long-term TANF. Form 8850 pre-screening within 28 days of hire + Form 5884 + Form 3800. Expired Dec 31 2025 — historically renewed; awaiting congressional extension.
view.s51.late_note: Missed 28-day Form 8850 deadline → NO CREDIT for this employee. Strict deadline, no retroactive cure. Set up dedicated WOTC process or use 3rd-party service (Walton, ADP, Equifax) to manage automatically on every hire.
view.s51.proc.28days: FILE Form 8850 + ETA 9061 with State Workforce Agency WITHIN 28 DAYS of hire
view.s51.proc.certified: Once certified, claim on Form 5884 + Form 3800 GBC
view.s51.proc.deny: Denial: no appeal, but may retry with different group
view.s51.proc.documentation: Documentation: DD-214 (veteran), public assistance records, ICE arrest record
view.s51.proc.missed: Missed 28-day: NO CREDIT for that employee — strict deadline
view.s51.proc.prescreen: Pre-screening: Form 8850 SIGNED BY EMPLOYEE on day of job offer
view.s51.proc.swa_review: SWA reviews + certifies (free) or rejects within 60-90 days
view.s51.proc.thirdparty: Third-party services: 0.5-2% of credit fee, manage 28-day deadline
view.s511.exc.bingo: Bingo / qualifying gaming (§ 513(f)) — primary purpose religious / charitable
view.s511.exc.convenience: Convenience for members / students / employees (§ 513(a)(2)) — cafeteria, bookstore
view.s511.exc.donated: Donated inventory: 100% inventory donated (§ 513(a)(3)) — thrift store
view.s511.exc.fundraising: Fundraising events: not regularly carried on if annual / occasional
view.s511.exc.mailings: Exchanges + rentals of mailing lists: NOT UBI (§ 513(h)(B))
view.s511.exc.publication: Distribution of low-cost items (§ 513(h)) — items ≤ $13.20 (2024 indexed)
view.s511.exc.research: Research income for federal / academic purposes: § 512(b)(8) exception
view.s511.exc.volunteer: Volunteer labor: substantially all (85%+) by volunteers (§ 513(a)(1))
view.s511.h1.title: // § 511 UBIT
view.s511.passive.controlled_org: Controlled organization income (§ 512(b)(13)): from > 50% owned sub may be UBI
view.s511.passive.debt_financed: EXCEPTION — DEBT-FINANCED PROPERTY (§ 514): UBI to extent of debt %
view.s511.passive.rents_personal: Personal property rents: ≤ 10% with real property OK; otherwise UBI
view.s511.passive.research_grants: Research grants from US gov / academic (§ 512(b)(7)-(9))
view.s511.silo.aggregate: Within same NAICS: aggregate income + expenses
view.s511.silo.investment_silos: Investment partnership K-1s: aggregate by "facts + circumstances"
view.s511.silo.naics: Trade or business defined by NAICS 2-digit code (Reg § 1.512(a)-6)
view.s511.silo.nol_per: NOLs: tracked per silo; cannot cross-silo
view.s511.silo.per_business: Compute UBTI separately for EACH trade or business
view.s511.silo.purpose: Prevent net loss in one UBI activity from offsetting income in another
view.s511.silo.qualified_partnership: "Qualifying partnership interest" (QPI): ≤ 2% direct OR ≤ 20% with no control = grouped
view.s511.silo.s990t_columns: Form 990-T: separate columns per silo with own loss carryover
view.s511.tax_note: UBIT TRIGGERED — file Form 990-T. Significant UBI may trigger IRS scrutiny of overall 501(c)(3) classification (if UBI is "substantial" portion of activity). Risk of revocation if UBI dominates. Consider spinning UBI activity into for-profit sub or partnership. § 512(a)(6) siloing limits cross-activity loss netting (TCJA 2017).
view.s511.test.aggregation: Aggregation: multiple unrelated activities considered separately (post-TCJA siloing)
view.s511.test.fragmentation: Fragmentation: bundled activities may be evaluated separately
view.s511.test.regular: REGULARLY CARRIED ON: continuous or recurring (vs occasional / one-time)
view.s511.test.related: If substantially related (gift shop in museum) → NOT UBTI
view.s511.test.s512a6: § 512(a)(6): separate computation per "trade or business" — defined per § 1.512(a)-6
view.s511.test.trade: TRADE OR BUSINESS: activity for production of income from sale of goods / performance of services
view.s511.test.unrelated: NOT SUBSTANTIALLY RELATED: causal connection to exempt purpose required (not just income generation)
view.s529.h1.title: // § 529 QUALIFIED TUITION PLAN
view.s529.hint.intro: Tax-free growth + tax-free withdrawals for qualified education expenses. 5-year super-funding: $90k single / $180k MFJ at once. State deductions vary — check residence-only vs any-plan. $10k/yr K-12 tuition qualified (since 2018). Post-2024 Roth IRA rollover up to $35k lifetime for accounts ≥ 15 yrs old.
view.s529.qual.elementary: NOTE: K-12 may NOT be state-qualified (only 30 states recognize)
view.s529.qual.room_board: Room + board (if half-time student, not exceeding school allowance)
view.s529.qual.student_loan: Up to $10k lifetime to student loan repayment (since 2019)
view.s529.qual.tuition: Tuition + required fees at eligible institution
view.s529.secure.body: Effective 2024: 529 account → Roth IRA rollover allowed when (1) account ≥ 15 yrs old, (2) within annual Roth contribution limit, (3) recipient must be 529 beneficiary, (4) cumulative $35,000 lifetime cap, (5) rolling over CONTRIBUTIONS (not earnings) made within 5 yrs prior NOT eligible. Eliminates over-funding concerns. Federal-only — state may not conform.
view.s530.h1.title: // § 530 COVERDELL ESA
view.s530.hint.intro: $2,000/yr per beneficiary cap (aggregate across all contributors). AGI phase-out: $95-110k single / $190-220k MFJ. Tax-free growth + tax-free withdrawals for qualified K-12 + higher-ed expenses. Contributor's AGI matters, NOT beneficiary's. Beneficiary must use by age 30 (or roll to family member). § 530 ESA + § 529 plan COMPLEMENTARY (use both); ESA covers broader K-12 expenses than 529.
view.s530.qual.higher_ed: Higher ed: tuition / fees / books / room+board (similar to 529)
view.s530.warning.18: Beneficiary age 18-30: contributions allowed only for special-needs beneficiaries. Must use balance by age 30 or transfer to family member.
view.s530.warning.30: Beneficiary age 30+: account must distribute within 30 days (taxable to extent of earnings + 10% penalty) OR transfer to family member < 30. Special-needs exception applies.
view.s59A.app.corp: Corporation (not S-corp, not RIC, not REIT)
view.s59A.app.derivatives: FDAP withholding NOT enough to escape BEAT
view.s59A.bep.deductible: Must give rise to a DEDUCTION (interest, royalties, services)
view.s59A.bep.related: Payments to FOREIGN RELATED PARTY (≥ 25% ownership)
view.s59A.bep.services_excluded: SCM (Services Cost Method) services without markup excluded
view.s59A.bep.tloan_relief: TLAC interest excluded for Globally Systemically Important Banks
view.s59A.h1.title: // § 59A BEAT
view.s59A.hint.intro: BEAT = 10% (post-2025: 12.5%) × MTI − regular tax. Applies to 'applicable taxpayer': large corp + base-erosion pct ≥ 3% (2% for banks + securities dealers) + avg gross receipts ≥ $500M (3-yr). MTI = TI + base-erosion tax benefits (deductible payments to foreign related parties). Defends US base from offshore deduction shifting. Filed on Form 8991.
view.s59A.note: BEAT triggered. Form 8991 required. Consider: SCM service restructuring, royalty rate adjustments, COGS recharacterization, qualified derivative payment election, intercompany loan refinancing to non-related sources.
view.s6011.cat.confidential: Confidential — fee paid + restriction on disclosure to others (Reg § 1.6011-4(b)(3))
view.s6011.cat.contractual: Contractual protection — refund/reduction if no tax benefit (Reg § 1.6011-4(b)(4))
view.s6011.cat.listed: Listed transactions — IRS-designated abusive (Notice 2009-7 etc., basket option contracts, Roth 401(k) etc.)
view.s6011.cat.loss: Loss transactions — $10M C-corp / $2M individual / $5M partnership / $50M for 5+ year (Reg § 1.6011-4(b)(5))
view.s6011.cat.s6707a_listed: Listed: 75% of decrease in tax / $200K cap / $100K min individual
view.s6011.cat.toi: Transactions of Interest — IRS designates as worthy of scrutiny (notice / rev. proc.)
view.s6011.f8886.advance_disclosure: Advance disclosure: file ahead of return — avoid some penalties
view.s6011.f8886.attached: Attached to return for year of participation
view.s6011.f8886.each_year: Filed EACH YEAR taxpayer continues to participate
view.s6011.f8886.first_year: Plus separate copy sent to OTSA (Office of Tax Shelter Analysis) for FIRST year
view.s6011.f8886.s6011_g: § 6011(g) failure to file = automatic § 6707A penalty (no reasonable cause for listed)
view.s6011.f8886.s6664_d: § 6664(d) — reasonable cause defense LIMITED for listed transactions
view.s6011.f8886.s6707a: § 6707A penalty: separate from accuracy-related, separate from interest
view.s6011.f8886.tax_benefit: Reports: category, transaction description, parties, projected tax benefit, fees paid
view.s6011.h1.title: // § 6011 REPORTABLE TRANSACTION DISCLOSURE (Form 8886)
view.s6011.hint.intro: Form 8886 reports participation in 5 categories of reportable transactions. § 6707A penalty up to $200K for listed transactions. § 6501(c)(10) extends SOL.
view.s6011.rel.circular_230: Circular 230 § 10.51 — practitioner discipline for false/fraudulent advice
view.s6011.rel.s6112: § 6112 — material advisor list maintenance ($10K/day after 20 day request)
view.s6011.rel.s6701: § 6701 — aiding understatement of liability ($1K/$10K per occurrence)
view.s6011.rel.s6707: § 6707 — material advisor penalty for failure to disclose
view.s6011.rel.s7525: § 7525 — federally authorized tax practitioner privilege LIMITED for tax shelters
view.s6011.sol.disclosure_protects: Timely Form 8886 disclosure: SOL runs normally from filing of underlying return
view.s6011.sol.purpose: Extends 3-year ASED to ONE year after Form 8886 filed (if late)
view.s6011.sol.s6501_a: § 6501(a) general 3-yr SOL starts from later of due date or actual filing
view.s6011.sol.s6501_e: § 6501(e) 6-yr SOL on 25%+ income omissions still applies
view.s6011.sol.s6664_b: § 6664(b) penalty SOL 3 years after disclosed transaction return filed
view.s6011.sol.unlimited: If never filed: ASED essentially UNLIMITED for that item
view.s6015.eq.legal_obligation: Legal obligation to pay (against if assumed)
view.s6015.h1.title: // § 6015 INNOCENT SPOUSE RELIEF
view.s6015.hint.intro: 3 forms: (b) Traditional Innocent Spouse, (c) Separation of Liability, (f) Equitable Relief. Relieves spouse of joint + several liability for tax on joint return attributable to other spouse. Form 8857. 2-year deadline for (b) + (c) from first IRS collection activity; (f) equitable within § 6502 CSED 10-yr period. Notice 2024-26 updated rules.
view.s6015.injured.body: Injured spouse: wants own SHARE of joint refund preserved when IRS plans to offset for spouse's separate debt (child support, student loans, back taxes). Filed simultaneously with return or up to 3 yrs after. Innocent spouse: wants relief from joint liability already assessed. Different forms, different facts, different timing.
view.s6015.label.knew: Knew or had reason to know?
view.s6015.row.b_who: Joint return + understatement from spouse + no knowledge + inequitable to hold liable
view.s6015.row.c_who: No longer married / separated 12+ mo; allocate as if MFS
view.s6015.row.f_who: Catch-all when (b)/(c) unavailable; Rev. Proc. 2013-34 weighing factors
view.s6015.warning.not_joint: Innocent spouse relief only applies to joint returns. Use Form 1040-X to amend or consider Equitable Relief if Form 8857 still timely.
view.s6033.dl.3_consecutive: 3 consecutive years failure: AUTOMATIC LOSS of exempt status
view.s6033.dl.990n: Form 990-N: filed online via IRS e-Postcard portal (free)
view.s6033.dl.extension: Form 8868 extension: 6-month automatic extension to November 15 (calendar)
view.s6033.dl.late_penalty: Late penalty: $20/day (up to $11K) or $100/day for large orgs (up to $56K)
view.s6033.dl.public_inspection: Public inspection: Forms 990 + 1023 available 3 yrs free at IRS
view.s6033.dl.reinstatement: Reinstatement via Form 1023 + Rev. Proc. 2014-11 streamlined process
view.s6033.dl.standard: Standard deadline: 15th day of 5th month after fiscal year end (May 15 for calendar)
view.s6033.exc.affiliate_of_church: Integrated auxiliary of church (e.g., school): NOT required to file (Form 4720 exception)
view.s6033.exc.below_minimum: Below $50K + first year: may still file Form 990-N for transparency
view.s6033.exc.exclusively_religious: Exclusively religious instructional orgs: NOT required to file
view.s6033.exc.govt_instrumentalities: Governmental units / instrumentalities: NOT required to file
view.s6033.exc.religious_orders: Religious orders / mission societies: NOT required to file
view.s6033.exc.unincorporated: Unincorporated 501(c)(3) under religious affiliation: may not need to file
view.s6033.exc.us_possessions: US possession affiliated orgs: limited filing
view.s6033.h1.title: // § 6033 EXEMPT ORG FILING
view.s6033.hint.intro: 501(c) orgs must file annual return: Form 990, 990-EZ, 990-N, or 990-PF. Form selection based on gross receipts + total assets. Form 990-N (e-Postcard): ≤ $50K gross receipts. Form 990-EZ: < $200K receipts + < $500K assets. Form 990 (full): ≥ $200K OR ≥ $500K. Form 990-PF: ALL private foundations. Failure 3 consecutive years: AUTOMATIC LOSS of exempt status (§ 6033(j) — Pension Protection Act 2006). Public inspection: Forms 990 publicly available.
view.s6033.revoked_note: AUTOMATIC REVOCATION: 3 consecutive years of failure to file. Exempt status LOST. Apply for reinstatement via Form 1023 + Rev. Proc. 2014-11 (streamlined available if eligible). Retroactive reinstatement possible if applied within 15 months of revocation notice. Public IRS list 'Auto Revocation' shows revoked orgs.
view.s6033.sb.501c3: 501(c)(3) public charity: only IRS sees names + addresses (post-2018 Rev. Proc. 2018-38)
view.s6033.sb.501c3_pi: Public inspection: amounts only (not donor names) for 501(c)(3) public charities
view.s6033.sb.501c4: 501(c)(4) + (5) + (6): NOT required (donor anonymity post-2018 Rev. Proc.)
view.s6033.sb.amat_v_irs: Americans for Prosperity v. Bonta (2021): SCOTUS struck down CA/NY donor disclosure
view.s6033.sb.federal_continue: Federal disclosure to IRS (not public): continues regardless
view.s6033.sb.pf: Private foundations: full disclosure even publicly (different rule)
view.s6033.sb.purpose: Discloses substantial contributors (≥ $5K AND ≥ 2% of total)
view.s6033.sb.state_rules: State-level: NY + CA stopped requiring after AFP v. Bonta
view.s6038.cat.1: US shareholder ≥ 10% in foreign passive investment company (§ 1297 PFIC overlap with 8621)
view.s6038.cat.2: US officer / director of foreign corp + US person owning ≥ 10%
view.s6038.cat.3: US person acquires ≥ 10% stock OR additional 5% during year
view.s6038.cat.5: US shareholder owning ≥ 10% of CFC at year end
view.s6038.h1.title: // § 6038 FORM 5471 CFC REPORTING
view.s6038.hint.intro: US person owning ≥ 10% of a CFC (foreign corp where US owners hold > 50%) must file Form 5471 with their return. Penalty: $10,000 per year per CFC for non-filing, escalating $10k/month up to $50k. Continuing failure reduces foreign tax credit. Subpart F + GILTI income flows through annually regardless of cash distribution. 5 categories of filer by ownership / control. Common trader trap: foreign holding companies.
view.s6038.label.name: Foreign corp name
view.s6038.stream.body: If you missed Form 5471 filings: Streamlined Domestic Offshore Procedures may allow late filing with reduced/waived penalties IF non-willful. Required: 3 years amended returns + 6 years FBARs + 5% offshore-account penalty (Domestic version). Foreign Streamlined: no penalty if non-resident. Voluntary Disclosure Program if willful: penalty up to 50% of high balance.
view.s6038.th.name: Name
view.s6038a.dis.body: Pre-2017: foreign-owned SMLLC = no US tax / filing if no ETB. Treas. Reg. § 301.7701-2 (final 2017) treats disregarded entity as separate corp for § 6038A reporting. Now required to file pro-forma 1120 + Form 5472. Doing nothing = $25k+ penalties per year.
view.s6038a.h1.title: // § 6038A FORM 5472 FOREIGN-OWNED US
view.s6038a.hint.intro: Required when (1) US corp with 25%+ foreign ownership, OR (2) foreign-owned single-member LLC (since 2017 disregarded entity rule). Reports related-party transactions in 11 categories. Penalty: $25,000 per failure (was $10k pre-2018) + $25k/month after 90 days. Common trap: foreign-owned US LLC holding US real estate. Pro-forma 1120 + Form 5472 required even for disregarded entity.
view.s6038a.rp.commonality: U.S. + foreign affiliates under common control
view.s6038a.rp.controlled: Any other foreign person controlled by 25% shareholder
view.s6038a.rp.cousins: Foreign brother-sister corps under common ownership
view.s6038a.rp.direct_25: Any 25% direct or indirect foreign shareholder
view.s6038a.rp.related_to_25: Any party related to 25% foreign shareholder under § 318 attribution
view.s6038a.warning.dis: Foreign-owned disregarded LLC (since 2017): MUST file pro-forma 1120 with Form 5472 attached. Common trap with foreign-owned single-member LLCs holding US real estate. Penalties stack year-over-year. No estate-tax / income filing history? Still required. File via 'Form 5472 only' return paperwork.
view.s6038b.form.5472: Form 5472: foreign-owned US corp / disregarded entity (§ 6038A)
view.s6038b.h1.title: // § 6038B FORM 926 FOREIGN TRANSFER
view.s6038b.hint.intro: US person transferring > $100k cash OR any property to foreign corp must file Form 926. Penalty: 10% of FMV (max $100k per failure; uncapped if intentional). § 367(a): US-built appreciation on transferred property triggers gain (foreign corp can't be tax shelter). § 367(d): intangibles get special 'deemed royalty' treatment. § 367(b): outbound reorganizations / spin-offs.
view.s6038d.ent.cfc: CFC reporting on Form 5471 separate from 8938
view.s6038d.ent.corp: Domestic corporations: ≥ 50% owned by US persons
view.s6038d.ent.partnership: Domestic partnerships: > 50% ownership by US persons
view.s6038d.ent.threshold_corp: Entity threshold: $50K end-year / $75K anytime (lower than individual)
view.s6038d.ent.trust: Domestic trusts: distribution power held by US person
view.s6038d.h1.title: // § 6038D FORM 8938 SPECIFIED FOREIGN ASSETS
view.s6038d.hint.intro: Form 8938 reports SFFA when value exceeds threshold. $50K-$600K depending on status + abroad. $10K base + $10K/30-day continuation. § 6662(j) 40% accuracy.
view.s6038d.pen.base: $10,000 base penalty for failure to file
view.s6038d.pen.continued: $10,000 per 30-day period after IRS notice (capped at $50,000)
view.s6038d.pen.crim_no: No criminal penalty under § 6038D itself (vs § 6038A potentially $25K)
view.s6038d.pen.fbar_overlap: FBAR penalties separate (FinCEN 114): up to $13,481 or 50% balance/willful
view.s6038d.pen.foreign_law: Foreign law non-disclosure does NOT establish reasonable cause
view.s6038d.pen.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause defense (§ 6038D(g)): "reasonable cause + not willful neglect"
view.s6038d.pen.s6501c8: § 6501(c)(8) ASED extension: indefinite for omitted income
view.s6038d.pen.s6662j: § 6662(j) 40% accuracy-related penalty on undisclosed SFFA understatement
view.s6038d.sffa.financial_account: Financial accounts maintained by foreign financial institution
view.s6038d.sffa.financial_instrument: Financial instrument or contract with foreign counterparty
view.s6038d.sffa.interest_foreign_entity: Interest in foreign entity (PFIC, foreign mutual fund, etc.)
view.s6038d.sffa.life_insurance: Life insurance contracts with cash value with foreign issuer
view.s6038d.sffa.stock_directly: Foreign stock or securities NOT held in financial account
view.s6039.espp.discount: 15% maximum discount on lower of: FMV at grant OR FMV at purchase
view.s6039.espp.disqualifying_treatment: Disqualifying disposition: discount + spread = ordinary income
view.s6039.espp.eligible: All 5%+ owner OR HCE-excluded employees may be excluded
view.s6039.espp.grant_holding: 2 years from beginning of offering period
view.s6039.espp.lookback: Lookback feature: discount based on lower of two prices
view.s6039.espp.qualifying_treatment: Qualifying disposition: discount = ordinary income up to gain, balance = LTCG
view.s6039.espp.s25k: $25,000 limit per offering period (FMV at grant basis)
view.s6039.espp.tax_form: W-2 reports compensation income on qualifying disposition
view.s6039.h1.title: // § 6039 ISO/ESPP INFORMATION REPORTING
view.s6039.hint.intro: Form 3921 (ISO) + Form 3922 (ESPP). Furnish to employee by Jan 31, file with IRS by Feb 28 paper / Mar 31 e-file. § 6721/6722 penalties up to $3.78M.
view.s6039.iso.amt_basis: AMT basis adjustment: increase by spread → reduces future AMT gain
view.s6039.iso.amt_credit: § 53 minimum tax credit allows future recovery of AMT paid
view.s6039.iso.amt_trap: AMT trap: § 56(b)(3) — spread at exercise included in AMTI
view.s6039.iso.both: BOTH must be satisfied for qualifying disposition
view.s6039.iso.disqual: Disqualifying disposition: ordinary income (FMV-strike) up to gain
view.s6039.iso.s100k: $100K rule: first $100K ISO grant per year eligible (excess = NSO)
view.s6039.iso.tax_qual: Tax treatment: 100% long-term capital gain on sale
view.s6039.tbl.aftaug1: Filed after Aug 1 or not at all
view.s6039.tbl.aug1: Filed after 30 days but by Aug 1
view.s6041.bw.b_notice: B-Notice / C-Notice: IRS sends to payer when discrepancy detected
view.s6041.bw.penalty: Payer penalty: 100% of unwithheld amount + interest if failure
view.s6041.bw.rate: Rate: 24% on REPORTABLE PAYMENT (since 2018 TCJA reduction from 28%)
view.s6041.bw.recipient_credit: Recipient credits BW against income tax on 1040 / 1120
view.s6041.bw.remit_945: Remit via Form 945 (Annual Return of Withheld Federal Income Tax)
view.s6041.bw.report_box4: Report on Form 1099 Box 4 (Federal Income Tax Withheld)
view.s6041.bw.triggers: Triggers: (1) no TIN provided, (2) incorrect TIN, (3) IRS notification (B-Notice)
view.s6041.bw.w9: Solution: get Form W-9 (Request for Taxpayer Identification Number)
view.s6041.bw_note: BACKUP WITHHOLDING REQUIRED — no W-9 / TIN from recipient. Withhold 24% from each payment + remit via Form 945 + report Box 4 of 1099. Recipient claims credit on income tax return. Failure to withhold: payer personally liable for entire amount. Obtain W-9 IMMEDIATELY before next payment.
view.s6041.dl.correction: Correction: file CORR 1099 promptly when error discovered
view.s6041.dl.efile_threshold: E-file mandatory: 10+ returns aggregate (post-2024 IRS regs)
view.s6041.dl.extension: Extension: Form 8809 (30 days automatic, may extend 30 more)
view.s6041.dl.misc_recipient: 1099-MISC: to recipient by January 31 (Boxes 8, 10 — by February 15)
view.s6041.exc.attorneys: EXCEPT: attorney fees (always reportable, even to law firm corp)
view.s6041.exc.corp_default: Corporations generally NOT subject to § 6041 reporting
view.s6041.exc.federal_govt: EXCEPT: federal executive agencies + federal contractors
view.s6041.exc.llc: LLCs: depends on TIN type (corp = exempt; partnership = reportable)
view.s6041.exc.s_corp: S-corps + LLCs (electing corp status): generally treated as corps
view.s6041.h1.title: // § 6041 INFO REPORTING (1099)
view.s6041.hint.intro: Payers must report $600+ payments to non-corp persons for services (NEC), rent, prizes, royalties (MISC). Threshold: $600 / yr per recipient. Form 1099-NEC (Box 1 nonemployee compensation) — since PATH Act 2020 separate from MISC. Form 1099-MISC (Boxes 1-14: rent, royalties, prizes, medical, attorney fees, etc.). Corporations exempt EXCEPT: attorneys, medical providers, federally-regulated. Backup withholding 24% if no TIN. Form 1096 transmittal.
view.s6041.pen.de_minimis: De minimis: 10 or less incorrect forms (or 1% of total) — no penalty
view.s6041.pen.intentional: Intentional disregard: $660 per form + UNLIMITED (no cap)
view.s6041.pen.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause exception: Boyle case strict (Form 1099 disputes rare)
view.s6041.pen.s6722: § 6722 statement to payee: similar penalties for failure to furnish
view.s6041.pen.small_biz: Small biz ($5M or less): reduced caps ($79K / $239K / $479K)
view.s6041.pen.tier1: Tier 1: $60 per form if corrected within 30 days (max $230K)
view.s6041.pen.tier2: Tier 2: $120 per form if corrected by August 1 (max $683K)
view.s6041.pen.tier3: Tier 3: $330 per form if NOT corrected by August 1 (max $1.37M)
view.s6045.bm.average_cost: Average cost: mutual funds only — historical default; locked once elected
view.s6045.bm.dividend_reinvestment: Dividend reinvestment: each share treated as separate lot
view.s6045.bm.election_method: Election made AT or BEFORE sale (per share lot) — broker confirms in writing
view.s6045.bm.fifo_default: FIFO: oldest lots sold first — DEFAULT for stocks if no election
view.s6045.bm.hifo: HIFO: tax-loss harvesting (highest basis first to maximize loss / minimize gain)
view.s6045.bm.lifo: LIFO: newest lots first (uncommon for equities, common bonds)
view.s6045.bm.specific_id: Specific identification: choose specific lot — REQUIRES BROKER CONFIRMATION at sale (settlement date)
view.s6045.bm.tax_lot_optimization: Tax lot optimization: most brokers offer auto-HIFO / 'max tax loss' mode
view.s6045.box_b_note: Box B (basis NOT reported to IRS): taxpayer must independently track + report basis. Common for: pre-2011 stock acquisitions, ESPP / RSU shares (often broker basis incorrect), inherited / gifted shares, transfers between brokers. Maintain personal records — IRS may audit if numbers diverge from broker reporting.
view.s6045.cov.bonds_2014: Less complex bonds: acquired AFTER 1/1/2014 — covered
view.s6045.cov.box_a: Box A: short-term basis reported to IRS
view.s6045.cov.box_b: Box B: short-term, basis NOT reported (taxpayer responsibility)
view.s6045.cov.box_d: Box D: long-term basis reported to IRS
view.s6045.cov.box_e: Box E: long-term, basis NOT reported
view.s6045.cov.complex_2016: Complex debt + options: acquired AFTER 1/1/2016 — covered
view.s6045.cov.digital_2025: Digital assets: brokers report effective 2025 (Form 1099-DA + Form 1099-DA-G)
view.s6045.cov.mutual_fund_2012: Mutual funds + ETFs: acquired AFTER 1/1/2012 — covered
view.s6045.cov.uncovered: Uncovered: pre-effective-date acquisitions OR transferred from non-broker
view.s6045.h1.title: // § 6045 BROKER REPORTING
view.s6045.hint.intro: Broker must report sales of securities + BASIS information to IRS + customer via Form 1099-B. Covered securities (since 2011): equities, mutual funds, options, futures, debt instruments. Basis methods: FIFO (default), LIFO, specific identification, average cost (mutual funds). Wash sale + dividend reinvestment + corporate action adjustments included. Box B: basis NOT reported to IRS (legacy / uncovered). § 6045(g): digital asset broker reporting (post-2023 Form 1099-DA).
view.s6045.recon.basis_difference: Basis difference from 1099-B: report adjustments + explanation
view.s6045.recon.code: Code column: adjustments codes (W wash sale, B basis adj, T term adj, etc.)
view.s6045.recon.cross_reference: Cross-reference: 1099-B box totals to Schedule D line totals
view.s6045.recon.estate_step_up: Inherited: § 1014 step-up to date-of-death FMV (often higher basis)
view.s6045.recon.gift: Gift: § 1015 carryover basis + dual basis rule
view.s6045.recon.schedule_d: Schedule D: net gain / loss from Form 8949
view.s6045.ws.30_days: Wash sale: buy 'substantially identical' within 30 days before / after sale at loss
view.s6045.ws.crypto: Crypto: NO wash sale rule currently (proposed legislation)
view.s6045.ws.disallowed: Loss DISALLOWED + ADDED to basis of replacement shares
view.s6045.ws.holding_period: Holding period of replacement shares TACKS from disallowed sale
view.s6045.ws.options_replacement: Option grant to buy same security: triggers wash sale
view.s6045.ws.same_broker_account: Within same account only — IRS Rev. Rul. 2008-5 (across accounts also)
view.s6045.ws.short_sales: Short sale closure triggers similar 30-day rule (Rev. Rul. 60-159)
view.s6045.ws.spouse_iras: Cross-account: IRA / 401(k) / spouse account also can trigger
view.s6045a.h1.title: // § 6045A COVERED SECURITY TRANSFER REPORTING
view.s6045a.hint.intro: Transferring broker must furnish basis statement to receiving broker within 15 days. Covered security phase-in 2011-2016. § 6722 penalty $60-$630/form.
view.s6045a.method.average: Average cost — only mutual funds / DRIPs eligible
view.s6045a.method.average_election: Average cost election: binding for that asset class + can change account-by-account
view.s6045a.method.fifo: FIFO (First In First Out) — default for equities
view.s6045a.method.high_low: "High cost" or "low cost" allowed within FIFO/specific ID framework
view.s6045a.method.spec_id: Specific ID: must identify before settlement date (Rev. Rul. 73-330)
view.s6045a.method.specific: Specific identification — must elect by sale date
view.s6045a.method.tax_loss_harvest: Tax loss harvesting: specific ID strongly favored for control
view.s6045a.rel.foreign: FATCA Chapter 4 withholding interacts with reporting
view.s6045a.rel.s1091: § 1091 wash sale — track 30-day window across accounts
view.s6045a.rel.s1259: § 1259 constructive sale of appreciated financial position
view.s6045a.req.method: Cost basis method (FIFO / specific ID / average)
view.s6045a.tbl.aftaug1: Furnished after Aug 1 or not at all
view.s6045a.tbl.aug1: Furnished after 30 days but by Aug 1
view.s6045b.act.cash_in_lieu: Cash in lieu of fractional shares
view.s6045b.act.stock_split: Stock splits, reverse splits, stock dividends > 5%
view.s6045b.broker.constructive: § 1259 constructive sale: separate broker reporting category
view.s6045b.broker.cov_uncov: 'Covered' vs 'non-covered' securities — only covered have basis reported
view.s6045b.broker.elect_default: Default cost method: FIFO; can elect HIFO / specific ID before sale
view.s6045b.broker.short_sale: Short sale basis: reporting begins year position closes
view.s6045b.broker.transfer: Transferring broker must send Form 1099-B + transfer statement within 15 days
view.s6045b.broker.wash_sale: Brokers must report wash-sale adjustments to basis
view.s6045b.cov.crypto_future: Crypto / digital assets: post-2025 reporting effective per IRA + IRS rules
view.s6045b.cov.dividends: Debt securities acquired after 1/1/2014 + complex debt after 1/1/2016
view.s6045b.cov.mutual_fund: Mutual fund + DRIP shares acquired after 1/1/2012
view.s6045b.h1.title: // § 6045B FORM 8937 COST BASIS
view.s6045b.hint.intro: Issuers must file Form 8937 within 45 days of any 'specified action affecting basis' — spinoffs, mergers, splits, mandatory exchanges. Public on issuer website. Brokers (§ 6045) report adjusted basis on 1099-B (covered stocks post-2011, mutual funds post-2012). Penalty: $310 per failure (2024); $630 if intentional. Annual cap: $3.78M. § 6045B-1(d) safe harbor: posting on website with 30-day notice to holders.
view.s6045b.label.notice: Notice provided to holders within 15 days?
view.s6048.excl.canadian_rrsp: Canadian RRSP / RRIF (Rev. Proc. 2014-55 auto exclusion)
view.s6048.excl.medical_education: Direct tuition / medical payments by foreign donor
view.s6048.excl.qualified_employee_trust: Qualified employee benefit trust (separate Form 8891 / FBAR)
view.s6048.h1.title: // § 6048 FORM 3520 FOREIGN TRUST / GIFT
view.s6048.hint.intro: Three reportable events: (1) transfer to foreign trust; (2) distribution from foreign trust; (3) receipt of > $100,000 gift from foreign individual / estate, OR > $19,570 (2024) from foreign corp / partnership. Penalty: 35% of unreported amount (5%/month, min $10k). Form 3520-A annually by trustee. U.S. owner of foreign grantor trust reports trust income on 1040 even if no distribution.
view.s6048.relief.delinquent: Delinquent International Information Return Submission Procedures (DIIRSP)
view.s6048.relief.precluded: Quiet disclosure / amended return — NOT penalty relief
view.s6048.relief.streamlined_dom: Streamlined Domestic Offshore (5% Title 26 miscellaneous penalty)
view.s6049.box.14: Box 14: tax-exempt + tax credit bond CUSIP
view.s6049.box.2: Box 2: early withdrawal penalty (deductible on Schedule 1)
view.s6049.box.9: Box 9: specified private activity bond interest (AMT preference)
view.s6049.exem.bank_dep_nra: Bank deposits to NRAs: § 871(i) exemption from 30% withholding (since 2013); still reported via 1042-S
view.s6049.exem.muni_exempt: Tax-exempt entities: NOT EXEMPT from reporting (but they still get 1099-INT)
view.s6049.exem.portfolio_int: § 871(h) portfolio interest: 0% withholding for foreign payees
view.s6049.exem.tin: Less than $10: no reporting (Box 1 + Box 3 combined)
view.s6049.exem.under_10: Threshold: Box 1 + Box 3 combined ≥ $10 → must file
view.s6049.h1.title: // § 6049 INTEREST REPORTING (1099-INT)
view.s6049.hint.intro: Payers must report $10+ interest paid + $600+ in trade/business interest. Form 1099-INT: Box 1 (interest income), Box 2 (early withdrawal penalty), Box 3 (US gov interest), Box 4 (federal withholding), Box 8 (tax-exempt interest), Box 9 (private activity bond interest — AMT prefer item). Form 1099-OID separate for original issue discount. Foreign payees: Form 1042-S separate regime (with W-8BEN). Bank deposit interest to NRAs: § 871(i) exemption from withholding (since 2013).
view.s6049.oid.box1: 1099-OID Box 1: OID amount accrued in current year
view.s6049.oid.de_minimis: De minimis OID: 0.25% × years to maturity → not reportable
view.s6049.oid.separate: OID accrual reported on separate Form 1099-OID, not 1099-INT
view.s6049.oid.short_term: Short-term obligations ≤ 1 year: no OID reporting
view.s6049.oid.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt municipal OID: excluded similar to regular muni interest
view.s6049.oid.taxpayer_pays: Taxpayer pays tax on accrual EVEN WITHOUT cash interest (zero-coupon problem)
view.s6049.oid.zero_coupon: Zero-coupon bonds: large OID accrual + no cash interest payments
view.s6049.pab_amt_note: Private Activity Bond (Box 9): federal tax-exempt for regular tax BUT AMT preference item adds back to AMTI. Watch out: high-income taxpayers w/ tax-exempt PAB income may unexpectedly hit AMT. Consider swapping to non-PAB muni bonds if AMT triggers consistently.
view.s6049.te.alternative_minimum: Pre-2018 individual AMT preference; post-2025 sunset uncertain
view.s6049.te.amt_pab: AMT preference: private activity bond interest (Box 9) — adds back for AMT
view.s6049.te.aotc: Tax-exempt interest affects: Social Security taxation, AOTC phaseout, IRA contribution limits
view.s6049.te.federal: Federal: municipal bond interest (Box 8) excluded from gross income
view.s6049.te.modified_agi: Modified AGI for ACA premium credit: includes tax-exempt interest
view.s6049.te.state_in_state: In-state municipal bonds: usually state tax exempt too
view.s6049.te.state_out_state: Out-of-state municipal: federal exempt + state TAXABLE (most states)
view.s6049.te.treasury_state: US Treasury / agency: state tax exempt (Box 3)
view.s6050W.exc.charity: Charitable contributions: excluded from PSE reporting (donor letter substantiates)
view.s6050W.exc.combined: Aggregation: PSE aggregates ALL transactions under SAME TIN/account
view.s6050W.exc.foreign: Foreign payees: not reportable (different reporting under FATCA / Ch 3 / 4)
view.s6050W.exc.personal: Personal transfers (gifts, splitting bills): NOT reportable; PSE must distinguish
view.s6050W.exc.refunds: Refunds + chargebacks: GROSS — taxpayer reconciles to net on Schedule C
view.s6050W.exc.s139_disaster: Disaster relief payments (§ 139): excluded from gross income, may still appear
view.s6050W.exc.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt entities: still receive 1099-K but typically excluded from UBI
view.s6050W.exc.under_threshold: Below threshold: PSE may STILL report (de minimis) — many file anyway
view.s6050W.h1.title: // § 6050W 1099-K REPORTING
view.s6050W.hint.intro: PSE (Payment Settlement Entity) reports gross payments via Form 1099-K. ARPA 2021 threshold $600 — Notice 2023-74 + 2024-85 delayed phase-in: $20K + 200 txns (2023, 2024 baseline) → $5K (2024) → $2.5K (2025) → $600 (2026). Affects: PayPal, Venmo, Cash App, Etsy, eBay, Stripe, Square, Airbnb sellers. Gross only — no expense deduction on form. Reconcile on Schedule C / Form 8949.
view.s6050W.personal_note: Personal transfers (friends + family): NOT taxable income. Reconcile on Schedule 1 — report Other Income gross then reverse with Other Adjustment 'Form 1099-K personal transfers not reportable'. PSE should have flagged as 'Personal Payment' but often misclassified.
view.s6050W.rec.airbnb: Airbnb / VRBO: Schedule E if not service business; Schedule C if substantial services
view.s6050W.rec.crypto: Crypto exchanges 1099-K: report on Form 8949 (specific identification by lot)
view.s6050W.rec.disagree: Disagree with PSE? Request CORR from PSE → Form 1040 reconciliation statement
view.s6050W.rec.friends: Friends + family Venmo: NOT income — but document and exclude on Schedule 1 adj
view.s6050W.rec.gig: Gig income: Schedule C if regular trade or Schedule SE for self-employment tax
view.s6050W.rec.personal_gain: Personal item GAIN: Schedule D — collectibles gain (Form 8949 capital gain)
view.s6050W.rec.personal_loss: Personal item LOSS (e.g., sold used couch): Schedule 1 → 'Other Income +X' then 'Other Adj -X'
view.s6050W.rec.schedule_c: Business income: Schedule C — gross 1099-K + COGS + expenses → net profit
view.s6051.box.1: Box 1: Wages, tips, other compensation (subject to federal income tax)
view.s6051.box.12: Box 12: Various codes (see list — most common)
view.s6051.box.13: Box 13: Statutory employee / retirement plan / third-party sick pay
view.s6051.box.3: Box 3: Social Security wages (up to wage base $176,100 for 2025)
view.s6051.dp.extension: Extension: Form 8809 (30-day automatic; only available if hardship)
view.s6051.dp.s6722: § 6722 penalty: failure to furnish to employee — same tiers
view.s6051.dp.ssa: Due to SSA: January 31 (paper + electronic) since 2017 PATH Act
view.s6051.dp.w3: Form W-3 transmittal: submit with paper W-2; not required for SSA electronic
view.s6051.h1.title: // § 6051 W-2 WAGE STATEMENTS
view.s6051.hint.intro: Employers furnish W-2 Wage + Tax Statement to each employee + Social Security Administration. Box 1: federal wages (taxable). Box 3 + 5: Social Security + Medicare wages. Box 12: codes for retirement (D, E, G), HSA (W), dep care (DD), group life > $50K (C), etc. Form W-3 transmittal. Due January 31 (employee + SSA, regardless of paper/electronic). SSA matching: wages cross-check vs SSA records.
view.s6051.label.s_corp_premium: S-corp health premium added to Box 1 ($)
view.s6051.pre.401k: 401(k) / 403(b) elective deferrals: reduce Box 1 but NOT Box 3/5 (FICA still applies)
view.s6051.pre.dep_care: Dependent care FSA: reduces Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 ($5K limit 2025)
view.s6051.pre.health: § 125 health insurance premiums: reduce Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 (full pre-tax)
view.s6051.pre.health_pre_tax_vs_post: After-tax health insurance: does NOT reduce — paid from net pay
view.s6051.pre.hsa: HSA contributions: reduce Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 (full pre-tax)
view.s6051.pre.imputed_income: Imputed income (group life > $50K): ADDS to Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5
view.s6051.pre.transit: Transit / parking: reduces Box 1 + Box 3 + Box 5 ($325/mo each 2025)
view.s6051.scorp.box_14: Optionally disclosed in Box 14 with code 'S CORP HEALTH'
view.s6051.scorp.deduction: Owner takes § 162(l) above-the-line deduction on personal return
view.s6051.scorp.health_taxable: Health insurance premiums INCLUDED in Box 1 (TAXABLE wages)
view.s6051.scorp.hsa: HSA: similar rule — included in Box 1, but excluded from Box 3/5 if HDHP
view.s6051.scorp.k1_split: Net effect: tax-free for FICA but income tax-free via § 162(l)
view.s6051.scorp.life_insurance: Group term life: similar inclusion rules
view.s6051.scorp.no_fica: Health premiums NOT in Box 3/5 (NO FICA on this portion)
view.s6051.scorp_note: 2%+ S-corp owner: health insurance premiums INCLUDED in Box 1 wages. NO FICA on health portion (Box 3/5 reduced). Owner takes § 162(l) above-the-line deduction on Form 1040. Net result: full income deductibility WITHOUT FICA. Box 14 disclosure recommended: 'S CORP HEALTH $X' for clarity.
view.s6072.ext.abroad: Abroad: automatic 2-month extension to June 15 — file by then OR file 4868 for additional 4 months
view.s6072.ext.combat: Combat zone: extension to 180 days after leaving zone
view.s6072.ext.disaster: Federally declared disaster: IRS announces specific extended dates per region
view.s6072.ext.entity: Entity: Form 7004 by original due date → automatic 6-month (5.5 for trust)
view.s6072.ext.individual: Individual: Form 4868 by April 15 → automatic 6-month extension to Oct 15
view.s6072.ext.no_combo: Extension does NOT extend SE tax payment due date
view.s6072.ext.pay_required: CRITICAL: extension to FILE only, NOT to pay — interest + penalty start from original due date
view.s6072.h1.title: // § 6072 RETURN DUE DATES
view.s6072.hint.intro: Individual: April 15 (calendar). C-corp: 15th day of 4th month after year-end. Partnership + S-corp: 15th day of 3rd month after year-end (March 15 calendar). Trust / estate: 15th day of 4th month (April 15 calendar). Extension: 6-month automatic via Form 4868 (individual) / Form 7004 (entities). Extension to FILE — NOT to PAY. Combat zone: 6+ months. Abroad: June 15 automatic for individuals.
view.s6072.late.6651a1: § 6651(a)(1) failure to file: 5% / month up to 25% of unpaid tax
view.s6072.late.6651a2: § 6651(a)(2) failure to pay: 0.5% / month up to 25% of unpaid tax
view.s6072.late.collection_alternative: Collection alternatives: installment agreement, OIC, CNC
view.s6072.late.first_time: First Time Penalty Abatement (FTA): one-time relief if compliant 3 prior yrs
view.s6072.late.interest: Interest at federal short-term rate + 3% (compounded daily)
