view.s6072.late.minimum: Minimum failure-to-file penalty: smaller of $485 (2024) or 100% of unpaid tax
view.s6072.late.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause exception: Boyle case very strict (limited to extraordinary circumstances)
view.s6072.unpaid_note: Unpaid balance + no extension: failure-to-file PLUS failure-to-pay penalties accrue from original due date. Combined penalty up to 47.5%. File ASAP even if can't pay. Form 9465 installment agreement available. First Time Penalty Abatement may waive if compliant 3 prior years.
view.s6111.cat.confidential: Confidential — fee paid + restriction on disclosure
view.s6111.cat.contractual: Contractual protection — refund if no tax benefit
view.s6111.cat.toi: Transactions of Interest — IRS designates as warranting scrutiny
view.s6111.coord.disclosure_taxpayer: Strategy disclosure may trigger client wave of audit + § 6662
view.s6111.coord.eited_taxpayer: Even if advisor fails to file 8918, taxpayer still required 8886
view.s6111.coord.injunction: § 7408 injunction available against repeat material advisor
view.s6111.coord.list_extension: List request triggers attorney-supervisor review at IRS national office
view.s6111.coord.sol_exception: § 6501(c)(10) extended SOL: 1 year after Form 8886 filed
view.s6111.coord.taxpayer: Taxpayer separately files Form 8886 (§ 6011) attached to return
view.s6111.h1.title: // § 6111 MATERIAL ADVISOR DISCLOSURE
view.s6111.hint.intro: Material advisor of reportable transaction must file Form 8918. § 6707 penalty: $50K (other) OR greater of $200K OR 75% gross income (listed/intentional). § 6112 list maintenance $10K/day.
view.s6111.list.contents: List: name, address, TIN, amount invested, copy of advice, all fees
view.s6111.list.no_cap: NO cap on total — can exceed $200K easily
view.s6111.list.penalty_clock: $10,000/day starts on day 21 — continues until full production
view.s6111.list.privilege: Attorney-client privilege NOT general defense — § 7525 narrow
view.s6111.list.production: Must produce within 20 business days of IRS written request
view.s6111.list.required: Material advisor must maintain list of advisees + transactions
view.s6111.list.retention: 7-year retention period from date last became material advisor
view.s6111.tbl.listed_tp: 75% of decrease in tax / $200K cap / $100K min
view.s6111.tbl.other_tp: 75% of decrease in tax / $50K cap / $10K min
view.s6111.thresh.cumulative: Cumulative across multiple participants in same transaction
view.s6111.thresh.expectation: Reasonably expects to receive — not actually received
view.s6111.thresh.listed_natural: Listed transaction, natural person: $50,000 gross income
view.s6112.contents.advice: COPY of all written advice provided
view.s6112.contents.fees: All fees received from each advisee
view.s6112.contents.name: Name + address of each advisee
view.s6112.contents.separate: Separate list for each REPORTABLE TRANSACTION category
view.s6112.coord.opr: OPR Circular 230 disciplinary proceedings — license sanctions
view.s6112.coord.s6701: § 6701 — aiding understatement of liability ($1K-$10K per occurrence)
view.s6112.coord.s6707: § 6707 — material advisor penalty for failure to file 8918
view.s6112.h1.title: // § 6112 MATERIAL ADVISOR LIST MAINTENANCE
view.s6112.hint.intro: Material advisor must maintain advisee list 7 years + produce within 20 business days of IRS request. § 6708 $10K/day penalty with NO CAP.
view.s6112.priv.attorney_client: Attorney-client: protects legal advice, NOT lists of clients
view.s6112.priv.crime_fraud: Crime-fraud exception: if used in furtherance of fraud (Notice 2009-7)
view.s6112.priv.identifying: Identifying clients NOT privileged in most circuits (Fisher v. US)
view.s6112.priv.in_camera: In camera review by judge possible (limited scope)
view.s6112.priv.privilege_log: Must provide privilege log + specific identification
view.s6112.priv.s7525_tax_shelter: § 7525 does NOT cover written tax shelter promotion
view.s6112.priv.work_product: Work product: NOT shielded for client lists or fee disclosures
view.s6112.prod.attorney_general: Attorney general may also pursue § 7402 summons
view.s6112.prod.compliance: Voluntary delivery within 21 days completely avoids penalty
view.s6112.prod.no_cap: NO CAP on penalty — can exceed $1M easily
view.s6112.prod.request: IRS written request from Office of Tax Shelter Analysis (OTSA)
view.s6112.prod.s6708_b: § 6708(b) — reasonable cause + good-faith effort defense available
view.s6159.def.csed_runs: IA running CSED restarts at termination
view.s6159.def.didnt_provide: Failed to provide requested financial info
view.s6159.def.estimated: Falls behind on estimated tax payments
view.s6159.def.new_balance: Owed new tax balance + didn't pay
view.s6159.h1.title: // § 6159 INSTALLMENT AGREEMENT
view.s6159.hint.intro: Pay debt monthly. Guaranteed IA: < $10k, 36 mo, automatic. Streamlined: < $50k, 72 mo. Routine: < $250k (post-2020). Non-streamlined: > $50k requires Form 433-F. Partial Pay IA (PPIA): reduced payment runs out CSED; debt forgiven at CSED. Setup fee: $22-$178 (waived low-income). Failure to pay = default.
view.s6166.acc.50_pct: ≥ 50% of qualifying business disposed of or withdrawn
view.s6166.acc.distribution: Undistributed net income retained (any amount triggers under § 6166(g)(2))
view.s6166.acc.distribution_corp: Corporate redemptions to pay estate tax (§ 303 carve-out limited)
view.s6166.acc.late: Late payment of installment after 6-month grace
view.s6166.h1.title: // § 6166 ESTATE TAX INSTALLMENT
view.s6166.hint.intro: Up to 14 years to pay federal estate tax on closely-held business interest. Years 1-4: interest-only at favorable 2% rate (on first $1.85M tax, 2024). Years 5-14: P+I. Closely-held = > 35% of adjusted gross estate must be active trade or business. Acceleration if 50% of business sold / undistributed income / late payment. Lien required; Form 706 election.
view.s6166.qual.aggregate: Aggregate test: combine multiple businesses (each ≥ 20% required)
view.s6166.qual.farm_business: Farms, ranches, family agricultural operations qualify
view.s6166.qual.holding_companies: Holding companies: must look through to operating sub
view.s6213.h1.title: // § 6213 NOTICE OF DEFICIENCY
view.s6213.hint.intro: IRS must issue 'Notice of Deficiency (90-day letter)' before assessment. 90 days to file Tax Court petition (150 days outside US). Petition SUSPENDS assessment + collection. Miss deadline = lose Tax Court rights. Alternative paths: pay + sue for refund (District Court / Court of Federal Claims) OR enter Appeals (administrative).
view.s6213.strat.appeals_first: Always try IRS Appeals BEFORE Tax Court (no cost, can settle)
view.s6213.strat.designated: Designated payment under § 6603 stops interest from running
view.s6213.strat.district_pros: District Court PROS: jury option, better for 'ordinary law' issues
view.s6213.strat.last_known_address: Notice valid if mailed to 'last known address' — even if returned
view.s6213.strat.payment_during: Payment during petition recharacterizes as deposit, refunded if win
view.s6213.strat.precedent: Choose forum based on most favorable PRECEDENT (DC Circuit favors gov't)
view.s6213.strat.statute_running: Petition timing = day 90 of notice (postmark counts for first-class mail)
view.s6213.strat.tax_court_pros: Tax Court PROS: don't pay first, suspend collection, expertise, small-case option
view.s6213.warning.urgent: URGENT: petition deadline approaching. File Tax Court petition NOW via TaxCourt.gov or pay deficiency + designate § 6603 deposit while pursuing claims.
view.s6221.aar.imputed: Imputed underpayment OR push-out election available
view.s6221.aar.no_partner_amend: Partners CANNOT file amended return for partnership items individually
view.s6221.aar.purpose: PARTNERSHIP-level amended return (replaces amended Schedule K-1)
view.s6221.aar.qualified_amended: Qualifies for § 6664(c) penalty relief (qualified amended return)
view.s6221.aar.s6227_b: § 6227(b) — special rules for partnership-level adjustments increasing income
view.s6221.aar.timing: Within 3 years of original return filing + before issuance of notice of audit
view.s6221.eo.bba_default: Default if no election: BBA centralized regime applies
view.s6221.eo.consequences: If elected: TEFRA-like partner-level audits — each partner's return adjusted separately
view.s6221.eo.disclosure: Election requires disclosure of each partner's TIN + ownership %
view.s6221.eo.election_annual: Election made ANNUALLY on timely-filed Form 1065 + Schedule B-2
view.s6221.eo.eligible: ALL partners ELIGIBLE: individuals, C-corps, S-corps, estates, foreign individuals, eligible foreign entities
view.s6221.eo.ineligible: INELIGIBLE: partnerships (any), non-grantor trusts, disregarded entities, foreign LLCs
view.s6221.eo.s_corp_count: S-corp counted as 1 partner BUT S-corp shareholders counted toward 100 limit
view.s6221.h1.title: // § 6221 BBA PARTNERSHIP AUDIT REGIME
view.s6221.hint.intro: Post-2017 default: entity-level audit + 37% imputed underpayment. § 6221(b) election out for ≤ 100 eligible partners. § 6226 push-out election within 45 days.
view.s6221.imp.adjustments: Net all partnership-level adjustments to reviewed year items
view.s6221.imp.interest: Interest from due date of reviewed year return
view.s6221.imp.modifications: § 6225 modifications reduce: amended partner returns + lower rates for specific partners
view.s6221.imp.netting: Netting only across SAME taxable year + partnership
view.s6221.pr.binding: PR's actions BIND partnership + all partners
view.s6221.pr.cannot_revoke: PR cannot revoke once designated except during audit (special procedure)
view.s6221.pr.designated_individual: If entity PR: must designate INDIVIDUAL to act for entity
view.s6221.pr.indemnification: Common: indemnification + insurance for PR liability
view.s6221.pr.individual_or_entity: Can be INDIVIDUAL or ENTITY (entity must have substantial US presence)
view.s6221.pr.no_partner_status: PR need NOT be partner — independent professional acceptable
view.s6221.pr.replaces_tmp: REPLACES TMP (Tax Matters Partner) in TEFRA
view.s6221.pr.s6223_b: § 6223(b) — IRS may designate PR if none designated
view.s6221.pr.single_contact: SINGLE point of contact with IRS
view.s6221.push.coordination: Often preferred where partners' actual tax rate < 37%
view.s6221.push.notice: Partnership issues PUSH-OUT STATEMENT to each reviewed-year partner
view.s6221.push.partner_character: Character preserved at PARTNER level (different from default § 6225)
view.s6221.push.partners_amend: Each partner files amended return for reviewed year (or pays tax via current year)
view.s6221.push.purpose: Shift tax liability from partnership to REVIEWED-YEAR partners
view.s6221.push.s6226_b: § 6226(b) — payable in CURRENT (adjustment) year by partners
view.s6221.push.s6226_c: § 6226(c) — partners include interest in adjustment year
view.s6221.push.s6226_d: § 6226(d) — penalties at partnership AND partner level
view.s6221.push.tiered: Tiered partnerships: passes through to UPPER partners
view.s6221.push.timing: Elected within 45 DAYS of final partnership adjustment
view.s6321.h1.title: // § 6321 FEDERAL TAX LIEN
view.s6321.hint.intro: Federal tax lien arises AUTOMATICALLY at assessment for tax + penalty + interest. Does NOT require NFTL filing. NFTL (Notice of Federal Tax Lien) sets PUBLIC priority vs other secured creditors. Attaches to ALL property + rights to property (Drye doctrine). Affects credit score until 10-yr CSED expiration or release. § 6325 options: release, discharge (specific property), withdrawal (false), subordination.
view.s6321.opt.cdp: CDP hearing (§ 6320): right to challenge NFTL within 30 days of filing
view.s6321.opt.compromise: Offer-in-Compromise § 7122 settles debt for less + lien release
view.s6321.opt.discharge: Discharge specific property (§ 6325(b)): pay equity attributable to that property
view.s6321.opt.equivalent: Equivalent hearing: post-30-day window, less powerful
view.s6321.opt.fresh_start: 'Fresh Start' 2011: NFTL threshold raised to $10k; withdrawal after full pay
view.s6321.opt.release: Release (§ 6325(a)): full payment OR CSED expired OR bond posted
view.s6321.opt.subordination: Subordination (§ 6325(d)): IRS gives priority to another lender
view.s6321.opt.withdrawal: Withdrawal (§ 6323(j)): erroneous filing OR not in best interest OR direct debit agreement
view.s6321.pri.45_day: 45-day window after NFTL: existing secured creditor still has priority
view.s6321.pri.mechanics: Mechanics lien + materialman lien generally retain priority
view.s6321.pri.personal: Personal property: file with state designation (UCC-like)
view.s6321.pri.purchaser: 'Purchaser' / 'holder of security interest' before NFTL = priority
view.s6321.pri.real_property: Real property: file with county recorder where situated
view.s6321.pri.super_priority: 'Super-priority' exceptions: § 6323(b) — wages, attorney fees, casualty insurance, etc.
view.s6325.app.administrative: Administrative appeals: Office of Appeals review
view.s6325.app.cdp: § 6320 CDP hearing: 30 days from Notice of Lien Filing
view.s6325.app.collateral: Collateral attack via refund suit limited
view.s6325.app.equivalent: Equivalent hearing (post-30-day): less powerful but available 1 yr
view.s6325.app.form_12277: Form 12277 — withdraw notice if benefits taxpayer (compliance / OIC / IA)
view.s6325.app.s6326: § 6326: 5 days to appeal erroneous filing — admin review
view.s6325.app.tax_court: Tax Court appeal after Final Notice of Determination
view.s6325.app.tax_court_lien: Tax Court can issue determination on lien filing appropriate
view.s6325.coll.s6321: § 6321: tax lien — attaches to all property + rights
view.s6325.coll.s6322: § 6322: lien arises at time of assessment
view.s6325.coll.s6323: § 6323: notice of lien — priority + protection for certain creditors
view.s6325.d.b1: § 6325(b)(1): RETAIN value ≥ 2× liability — value safely covers exposure
view.s6325.d.b2: § 6325(b)(2): VALUELESS to government — no IRS interest in property
view.s6325.d.b3: § 6325(b)(3): BOND or cash deposit — substitute security
view.s6325.d.process_time: Typical: 30-60 days to process discharge application
view.s6325.d.refinance: Refi: § 6325(d) subordination allows new lien priority
view.s6325.d.sale: Sale of property: IRS interest follows proceeds (Subordination)
view.s6325.delay_note: Request pending > 30 days. Contact IRS Centralized Lien Office (CLO) at 800-913-6050. Form 911 to Taxpayer Advocate Service if hardship. CDP hearing may be available if appeal rights not exhausted. Consider local TAS if facing financial harm.
view.s6325.h1.title: // § 6325 LIEN RELEASE
view.s6325.hint.intro: Lien RELEASED when: liability satisfied, uncollectible, OR bond posted. § 6325(a)(1): full payment / accepted Offer in Compromise. § 6325(b)(1): partial release of specific property — value retained ≥ 2× liability. § 6325(b)(2): discharge if government's interest valueless. § 6325(b)(3): bond / cash deposit. § 6325(d): subordination for refinancing. § 6326: 5-day appeal of erroneous filing.
view.s6325.r.bonded: Bond posted equal to liability (Form 12153 if appealing)
view.s6325.r.paid: Full payment of liability + interest + penalties
view.s6325.r.refile: Refile possible if facts change OR new period within statute
view.s6325.r.self_releasing: Self-releasing lien: certain liens release automatically (Form 668(Y))
view.s6325.r.timing: Release issued within 30 days of request / qualifying event
view.s6325.r.uncollectible: CNC (Currently Not Collectible) status + lien hindering collection
view.s6330.coord.audit_recon: Audit reconsideration: separately request review of underlying tax
view.s6330.coord.bankruptcy: Bankruptcy filing: automatic stay (§ 362) suspends collection + CDP
view.s6330.coord.equivalent: Equivalent hearing: missed 30-day window — within 1 year possible (NO Tax Court review)
view.s6330.coord.federal_employee_pay: Federal employee pay levy: NO CDP for some IRS-determined cases
view.s6330.coord.jeopardy: Jeopardy levy: § 6330(f) NO CDP — § 7429 separate review
view.s6330.coord.s6320: § 6320 — parallel CDP for LIEN notice (within 5 business days of NFTL)
view.s6330.coord.s6502: § 6502 — collection SOL (10 years) TOLLED during CDP + appeal
view.s6330.coord.s7345: § 7345 passport revocation for seriously delinquent tax debts ($62K+ in 2024)
view.s6330.coord.state_levy: State refund levy: NO CDP (§ 6330(f) exception)
view.s6330.coord.tco: Taxpayer Advocate Service (TAO) — collaborative review when systemic burden
view.s6330.h1.title: // § 6330 CDP LEVY HEARING
view.s6330.hint.intro: 30-day notice before levy. Form 12153 within 30 days. Independent Appeals officer. Tax Court jurisdiction. Levy suspended + § 6502 collection SOL tolled.
view.s6330.m.appropriate: Appropriateness of collection action — alternatives vs intrusion
view.s6330.m.spousal_relief_first_time: Spousal defense allowed even if previously could have raised
view.s6330.m.under_liab_test: "Prior opportunity" = received Statutory Notice of Deficiency OR participated in pre-assessment audit
view.s6330.m.underlying: Challenges to underlying liability — ONLY if no prior opportunity
view.s6330.proc.30_days: Taxpayer has 30 DAYS from notice DATE to request CDP hearing
view.s6330.proc.appeals: Appeals officer conducts hearing — taxpayer can raise § 6330(c) matters
view.s6330.proc.collection_resumes: If no petition or after Tax Court rules: collection action may resume
view.s6330.proc.f12153: File Form 12153 — Request for Collection Due Process Hearing
view.s6330.proc.notice: IRS issues Final Notice of Intent to Levy + Right to a Hearing (Letter 1058 / CP90 / Letter 11)
view.s6330.proc.notice_det: Notice of Determination issued by Appeals — concludes hearing
view.s6330.proc.petition_30: 30 days from Notice of Determination to petition Tax Court (§ 6330(d))
view.s6330.proc.sol_tolled: Collection SOL (§ 6502 — 10 years) is TOLLED during hearing + appeal
view.s6330.proc.suspension: Filing SUSPENDS collection (no levy) until hearing complete + appeals exhausted
view.s6330.proc.tax_court: Tax Court hearing — reviews CDP determination
view.s6330.std.abuse: "Abuse of discretion" — standard for collection alternatives, spousal defenses, appropriateness
view.s6330.std.administrative_record: Administrative record review (no new evidence outside hearing typically)
view.s6330.std.davis: Davis v. Comm. — Tax Court reviews Appeals' OIC denial for abuse of discretion
view.s6330.std.de_novo: "De novo" — review of underlying liability (when properly at issue)
view.s6330.std.factors: Abuse of discretion factors: arbitrary, capricious, without rational basis
view.s6330.std.lunsford: Lunsford v. Comm. — equivalent hearing not subject to Tax Court review (vs CDP)
view.s6330.std.s6330_c_2_a: § 6330(c)(2)(A)(i) — verification IRS followed all law + admin procedure
view.s6330.std.s6330_c_3: § 6330(c)(3) — Appeals officer required to balance need for collection vs intrusion
view.s6330.std.thomas: Thomas v. Comm. — taxpayer must offer alternative + cooperate (cannot simply refuse)
view.s6330.tbl.ia_note: Streamlined ≤$50K / Direct Debit / Partial Pay IA
view.s6330.tbl.oic_note: Doubt as to liability / collectibility / ETA
view.s6331.ex.basic_living: Basic living standard (per IRS exemption tables)
view.s6331.ex.fuel: $13,510 (2024) of fuel + provisions + furniture + personal effects
view.s6331.ex.social_security: Social Security: only 15% can be levied (FPLP automated levy)
view.s6331.h1.title: // § 6331 LEVY / WAGE GARNISHMENT
view.s6331.hint.intro: Levy seizes property after notice + demand + Final Notice of Intent + 30 days. CDP right under § 6330: 30 days to request hearing → SUSPENDS collection. Exemptions: tools of trade ($5,540 2024), unemployment, child support, basic living standard. Continuous wage levy takes everything ABOVE exempt amount (Form 668-W). Bank levy is ONE-TIME (snapshot at receipt).
view.s6331.res.cdp: CDP hearing within 30 days of Final Notice (Form 12153)
view.s6331.res.equivalent: Equivalent hearing (post-30-day) Form 13753 — less powerful but available
view.s6331.res.release: Request levy release: Form 911 or Taxpayer Advocate
view.s6331.warning.cdp_urgent: URGENT: Final Notice (LT11) received. File Form 12153 within 30 days to request CDP hearing — preserves all collection alternatives + Tax Court review of any determination. Missing window means equivalent hearing only.
view.s6404.e.18_months: Significant delay: typically 18+ months as benchmark
view.s6404.e.discretionary: DISCRETIONARY — IRS may abate "any portion attributable to" delay
view.s6404.e.f843: Form 843 — attach detailed explanation of IRS delay
view.s6404.e.factors: Factors: cause of delay, IRS responsibility, taxpayer cooperation
view.s6404.e.managerial: Managerial act: lost file, supervisor approval, internal review delays
view.s6404.e.ministerial: Ministerial act: typing, computation, routine processing
view.s6404.e.no_substantive: NOT applicable: substantive legal decisions, audit position changes
view.s6404.e.scope: Abate INTEREST attributable to IRS error/delay (managerial / ministerial act)
view.s6404.e.tax_court: § 6404(h) Tax Court review post-1996 (abuse of discretion)
view.s6404.f.complete: Taxpayer provided COMPLETE + ACCURATE information when seeking advice
view.s6404.f.f843_with_supporting: Form 843 + copies of original request + advice received
view.s6404.f.scope: Abate interest + penalty arising from reliance on written IRS advice
view.s6404.f.scope_advice: Limited to advice from IRS DIRECTLY (not website / publications)
view.s6404.f.written: Must be WRITTEN advice (oral advice insufficient)
view.s6404.fta.criteria: Eligibility: NO penalties in PRIOR 3 years + currently in compliance + filed all required returns
view.s6404.fta.first_time_only: First-time only — cannot use again for same penalty type
view.s6404.fta.no_reasonable: No reasonable cause required — automatic 1-time waiver
view.s6404.fta.request: Request via Form 843, phone, or written letter
view.s6404.g.30_day_letter: 30-day letter or revenue agent report counts
view.s6404.g.commencement: Suspension period: from day after due date until 18 days after notice
view.s6404.g.purpose: Suspends interest + penalty if IRS doesn't notify within 36 MONTHS
view.s6404.g.s6404_g_2_a: § 6404(g)(2)(A) — extensions of time (no suspension during extension period)
view.s6404.g.scope: Applies to: § 6651 FTP + interest on amount in notice
view.s6404.g.timely: Critical for taxpayers receiving long-delayed adjustment notices
view.s6404.h1.title: // § 6404 ABATEMENT OF TAX / INTEREST / PENALTIES
view.s6404.hint.intro: § 6404(e) IRS delay 18+ mo + § 6404(f) erroneous written advice + § 6404(g) 36-mo notice failure suspension. Form 843. First-Time Penalty Abatement (FTA) IRM.
view.s6404.proc.cdp_coordination: § 6320 / § 6330 CDP hearing can include abatement requests
view.s6404.proc.documentation: Attach: detailed explanation, supporting documents, copies of relevant correspondence
view.s6404.proc.f843: Form 843 — claim for refund or request for abatement
view.s6404.proc.s7430: § 7430 attorney fees if prevailing on abuse of discretion claim
view.s6404.proc.sol_refund: § 6511 SOL for refund claims: 3 yrs from return filing OR 2 yrs from payment
view.s6404.proc.tao: Taxpayer Advocate Service (TAS) when systemic burden or unreasonable delay
view.s6404.proc.tc_review: § 6404(h) Tax Court review of § 6404(e) denial — 180-day deadline
view.s6404.rc.boyle: United States v. Boyle (1985): cannot rely on agent for filing
view.s6404.rc.complete_disclosure: Complete disclosure of facts to advisor
view.s6404.rc.factors: Factors: serious illness, family death, natural disaster, fire, etc.
view.s6404.rc.reliance_on_advisor: Reliance on tax advisor for SUBSTANTIVE issue (not filing)
view.s6502.h1.title: // § 6502 CSED 10-YR COLLECTION SOL
view.s6502.hint.intro: IRS must collect within 10 years of assessment. After CSED: debt legally UNCOLLECTIBLE (lien auto-releases). Suspensions extend CSED: OIC pending, CDP hearing, bankruptcy (+ 6 mo), military combat, IA pending, Innocent Spouse application (+ 60 days each). Verify dates via Form 4340 (account transcript). Misjudgment by IRS happens — fight for the original CSED.
view.s6502.strat.cnc: Currently Not Collectible (CNC) status preserves CSED running
view.s6502.strat.fpa: File-then-pay: Get-out-of-jail-free if pay before CSED but it just expired
view.s6502.strat.no_borrow: Don't borrow money to pay near-CSED debt
view.s6502.strat.no_ia: DON'T enter IA in final year — extends CSED
view.s6502.strat.no_oic: DON'T file OIC just before CSED — extends collection time
view.s6502.strat.partial_pay: Partial-pay IA: CSED still runs, debt forgiven at CSED
view.s6502.strat.review_transcript: Review Form 4340 annually — IRS sometimes miscalculates CSED
view.s6502.strat.run_out_clock: Run out the clock: hold tight + comply with current filing requirements
view.s6502.toll.cdp: § 6320/§ 6330 CDP hearing: from CDP request through final determination + appeals
view.s6502.toll.ia: Installment agreement pending: from submission + 30 days after termination
view.s6502.toll.innocent_spouse: § 6015 Innocent Spouse election + 60 days after IRS final determination
view.s6502.toll.oic: § 7122 OIC pending: pendency + 30 days after rejection/withdrawal
view.s6502.toll.signed_extension: § 6502(a)(2) waiver extension signed by taxpayer (RARE; avoid)
view.s6601.abatement.f843: Form 843 — claim for abatement of interest/penalties
view.s6601.abatement.factors: Factors: tax type, year, IRS delay attributable
view.s6601.abatement.s6404_e_1: § 6404(e)(1) — discretionary, IRS may abate "any portion" attributable to delay
view.s6601.abatement.s6404_h: § 6404(h) — Tax Court jurisdiction over abatement denial
view.s6601.abatement.scope: IRS delays of 18+ months in audit + collection (limited)
view.s6601.abatement.unilateral: Court review of IRS denial limited (abuse of discretion standard)
view.s6601.calc.daily: DAILY COMPOUND from due date until paid
view.s6601.calc.rate_change: Rate changes quarterly — must use applicable rate per period
view.s6601.calc.s6621_d_netting: § 6621(d) interest netting: simultaneous over + under → use overpayment rate
view.s6601.h1.title: // § 6601 INTEREST ON UNDERPAYMENTS
view.s6601.hint.intro: Daily compound interest from due date. § 6621(a)(2) ST + 3pp underpayment / § 6621(c) hot corp ≥ $100K = ST + 5pp. § 6404(e) IRS-delay abatement.
view.s6601.net.amount: Lesser of (overpayment balance) AND (underpayment balance) → net at OVERPAYMENT rate
view.s6601.net.application_request: Must claim netting via formal request — not automatic
view.s6601.net.benefit: Reduces interest exposure by ~1-3pp × balance × years
view.s6601.net.purpose: Eliminates rate differential when taxpayer has simultaneous over + under
view.s6601.net.refund_claim: Often claimed via refund claim + Form 843
view.s6601.net.scope: Applies when: same period + both balances exist on books
view.s6601.timing.amended: Amended return: interest from original due date
view.s6601.timing.due_date: § 6601(a) interest from "last date prescribed for payment"
view.s6601.timing.extension: Extension to file ≠ extension to pay (interest from original due)
view.s6601.timing.ftc: § 6601(e)(3) FTC: interest accrues for portion attributable to FTC limitation
view.s6601.timing.nol_carryback: § 6601(e)(1) NOL carryback: interest only AFTER loss year filing
view.s6601.timing.s6601_b_3: § 6601(b)(3) — payments applied as of received date
view.s6601.timing.transcript: Account transcript may show daily interest accruals
view.s6651.h1.title: // § 6651 FAILURE TO FILE / PAY
view.s6651.hint.intro: (a)(1) Failure to file: 5%/month of unpaid tax, max 25%. Min $485 (2024) if > 60 days late. (a)(2) Failure to pay: 0.5%/month, max 25%. Combined: FTF rate REDUCED to 4.5%/month when both apply (so combined never exceeds 5%/month). FRAUDULENT FTF: 15%/month, max 75%. First-Time Abatement available for compliant taxpayers (penalty once / 3 yrs).
view.s6651.rel.disaster: Federally-declared disaster: automatic extension per IRS announcement
view.s6651.rel.fta: First-Time Abatement: clean 3-yr record + currently filing/paying compliance
view.s6651.rel.ia_ftp: FTP rate halved (0.25%/mo) during installment agreement period
view.s6651.rel.ignorance: Ignorance of law NOT reasonable cause (United States v. Boyle 1985)
view.s6651.rel.no_fta_fraud: FTA + reasonable cause NOT available for fraudulent FTF
view.s6651.rel.reasonable: Reasonable cause: serious illness, natural disaster, records destroyed, IRS error
view.s6651.rel2.6651_d: § 6651(d): rate INCREASED to 1%/month after IRS issues 10-day notice + levy threat
view.s6651.rel2.6651_h: § 6651(h): rate REDUCED to 0.25%/month during installment agreement
view.s6651.rel2.6656: § 6656 Failure to deposit (employer trust fund taxes — 2-15% by lateness)
view.s6651.rel2.6672: § 6672 Trust Fund Recovery Penalty (TFRP) — 100% of unpaid trust fund taxes
view.s6654.h1.title: // § 6654 ESTIMATED TAX SAFE HARBOR
view.s6654.hint.intro: Safe harbors avoid underpayment penalty: (1) 90% of current-year tax OR (2) 100% of prior-year tax (110% if prior AGI > $150k). Quarterly due: Apr 15, Jun 15, Sep 15, Jan 15. Withholding is treated as paid evenly across the year. Penalty = federal short-term AFR + 3% per quarter on each quarter's shortage. Farmers / fishermen: 66.67% safe harbor + Jan 15 single-date pay.
view.s6654.work.annualized: Form 2210 Schedule AI: annualized income method for lumpy / Q4-loaded income
view.s6654.work.farmer: Farmer / fisherman: pay 66.67% by Jan 15 OR full tax by Mar 1, no quarterly
view.s6654.work.first_year: First-year filer with no prior-year liability: no penalty
view.s6654.work.january: January 15 final due date — pay shortage then
view.s6654.work.withhold_dec: Increase W-2 withholding in December — treated as paid evenly across year
view.s6655.ann.elections: 3 different election periods available (months may vary)
view.s6655.ann.purpose: Purpose: avoid penalty when income SPIKED in later periods
view.s6655.ann.q2: Q2: annualize Jan-May × 12/5 → tax × 50%, minus Q1
view.s6655.ann.q3: Q3: annualize Jan-Aug × 12/8 → tax × 75%, minus Q1+Q2
view.s6655.ann.q4: Q4: annualize Jan-Nov × 12/11 → tax × 100%, minus Q1+Q2+Q3
view.s6655.ann.recalculation: Each quarter rechecked — late-year underpayment may trigger earlier penalty
view.s6655.annualized_note: Annualized income method elected: payment requirements based on YTD income annualized quarterly, not flat 25%. Particularly valuable for cyclical businesses w/ Q4 spikes. Form 2220 Part III computation. Reduces Q1-Q3 underpayment but Q4 cumulative test catches up if not paid sufficiently.
view.s6655.h1.title: // § 6655 CORP ESTIMATED TAX
view.s6655.hint.intro: Corp must pay estimated tax in 4 installments (4/15, 6/15, 9/15, 12/15) totaling 100% of expected tax. Underpayment penalty: federal short-term rate + 3% on shortfall (Form 2220). Safe harbors: (1) 100% of prior-year tax (large corp: 100% of CURRENT year), (2) annualized income method, (3) adjusted seasonal installment. Large corp: avg taxable income ≥ $1M in any of past 3 yrs — ONLY current-year safe harbor available. Form 1120-W worksheet (not filed).
view.s6655.pen.exception_first: First-year corp + small operations: no penalty under § 6655(g)
view.s6655.pen.from: Period: due date of installment to earlier of payment OR Form 1120 filing
view.s6655.pen.reasonable_cause: Reasonable cause exception: rarely granted (Boyle case strict)
view.s6655.sched.consolidated: Consolidated group: parent + all subs aggregate
view.s6655.sched.equal: Equal installments OR annualized / seasonal methods
view.s6655.sched.q1: Q1: April 15 — 25% of required annual payment
view.s6655.sched.weekend: If due date is weekend / holiday: next business day
view.s6662.def.competent: Advisor must be competent + provided ALL relevant facts
view.s6662.def.limit_tax_shelter: LIMITED for tax shelters (§ 6662(d)(2)(C)): must satisfy MLTN standard
view.s6662.def.qualified_amended: Qualified amended return (filed before audit notice) avoids penalty
view.s6662.def.reasonable_basis: Reasonable basis: less than substantial authority but reasonable (20-30% chance)
view.s6662.def.reliance: Reliance on professional advice: Boyle factors + specific issue analyzed
view.s6662.def.s6662_h_no_defense: § 6662(h) NO § 6664 defense for gross valuation misstatement (post-PPA)
view.s6662.def.s6664_d: § 6664(d) — defense LIMITED for listed transactions / reportable transactions
view.s6662.def.s6664_d_must: Must show: (a) full disclosure + (b) substantial authority + (c) reasonable belief MLTN
view.s6662.def.subst_auth: Substantial authority: more likely than not (40%+ chance of prevailing)
view.s6662.h1.title: // § 6662 ACCURACY-RELATED PENALTY (20%/40%)
view.s6662.hint.intro: 20% on negligence + substantial understatement + valuation misstatement. 40% gross valuation + foreign asset undisclosed. § 6664 reasonable cause defense limited for tax shelters.
view.s6662.s6751.belair_w: Belair Woods v. Comm. — Form 30-day letter triggers § 6751(b)
view.s6662.s6751.graev: Graev v. Comm. + Chai v. Comm. + Laidlaw — judicial scrutiny
view.s6662.s6751.kestin: Kestin v. Comm. — initial determination = formal communication to taxpayer
view.s6662.s6751.litigation: Litigation tactic: penalties often vacated where § 6751(b) not satisfied
view.s6662.s6751.required: Written supervisor approval required BEFORE penalty assessed
view.s6662.s6751.timing: Approval must be made before initial determination communicated
view.s6662.su.amounts: Computed on UNDERSTATEMENT before adjustment for substantial authority + disclosure
view.s6662.su.corp_under_eligible: C-corp (NOT S-corp): LESSER of $10M OR 10% of correct tax
view.s6662.su.individual: Individual: GREATER of $5,000 OR 10% of correct tax
view.s6662.su.s6662_d_2_a: § 6662(d)(2)(A) — items WITH substantial authority excluded from understatement calculation
view.s6662.su.s6662_d_2_b: § 6662(d)(2)(B) — items WITH disclosure + reasonable basis excluded
view.s6662.su.s6662_d_2_c: § 6662(d)(2)(C) — tax shelter items require MLTN substantial authority
view.s6662.su.s_corp: S-corp / partnership: tested at INDIVIDUAL level on K-1 items
view.s6662.su.subst_auth_examples: Substantial authority sources: Code, regs, court cases, IRS publications, revenue rulings
view.s6662.val.basis_step_up: Built-in gain valuations on basis step-ups
view.s6662.val.charitable: Charitable contribution: appraisal required for $5K+ — failure invalidates substantial authority
view.s6662.val.estate_gift: Estate/gift: 65% or less of correct value (substantial) / 40% (gross)
view.s6662.val.fair_market: FMV determined as of relevant date
view.s6662.val.qualified_appraisal: Qualified appraisal required for charitable contributions (Reg § 1.170A-13)
view.s6662.val.s_substantial: SUBSTANTIAL: 150% or more of correct value (post-PPA 2007 — was 200%)
view.s6663.badge.consciousness_guilt: 'Consciousness of guilt' behaviors (rescheduling, evading IRS contacts)
view.s6663.badge.evasion: Repeated underpayment + filing failures over multiple years
view.s6663.h1.title: // § 6663 CIVIL FRAUD 75%
view.s6663.hint.intro: 75% of underpayment portion due to fraud. IRS proves by clear + convincing evidence. Once any fraud established, ENTIRE underpayment presumed fraudulent (rebuttable by taxpayer's evidence). No SOL limit (§ 6501(c)(1)). 0.5%/month interest. Civil vs criminal: § 6663 civil; § 7201 criminal (5-yr felony) — government usually wins civil first then indicts.
view.s6663.label.correctly: Correct tax that should have been reported ($)
view.s6663.rel.6651: § 6651 Failure to file: 5%/month up to 25% (or 15%/month if fraudulent)
view.s6663.rel.6694: § 6694 Return preparer: $5k or 75% of preparer's fee for unreasonable position
view.s6664.boyle.body: Reliance on agent for FILING DEADLINE = NOT reasonable cause (Boyle). However, reliance on advice for SUBSTANTIVE TAX positions CAN be reasonable cause if Neonatology factors met. Distinction: 'ministerial / clerical tasks' (taxpayer responsible) vs 'substantive judgment' (reliance OK). 7th Circuit in West v. Comm'r reaffirmed but with nuanced application.
view.s6664.fac.adverse_circumstances: Position taken in adverse-circumstance avoidance pattern
view.s6664.fac.expert_taxpayer: Taxpayer is professional / expert with knowledge
view.s6664.fac.large_amount: Large dollar amount of underpayment relative to total income
view.s6664.fac.late_response: Late response to IRS inquiries / non-cooperation
view.s6664.fac.willful_blindness: Willful blindness — reckless disregard for facts
view.s6664.h1.title: // § 6664 REASONABLE CAUSE DEFENSE
view.s6664.h2.reliance_factors: Valid professional-reliance criteria (Neonatology Associates v. Comm'r)
view.s6664.hint.intro: Statutory defense against § 6662 accuracy + § 6663 civil fraud penalties. Requires (1) reasonable cause + (2) good faith. Burden on TAXPAYER. Factors: effort to assess proper liability, knowledge / experience, reliance on professional advice (with valid-reliance criteria). Doesn't apply to § 6707A reportable transaction penalty (strict liability).
view.s6664.rel.boilerplate: Boilerplate opinions / kit-style memoranda generally don't qualify
view.s6664.rel.discussion: Substantive discussion of facts + applicable law occurred
view.s6664.rel.good_faith_reliance: Taxpayer actually relied in good faith on adviser's judgment
view.s6664.rel.independence: Adviser had no inherent conflict of interest / lack of independence
view.s6664.rel.qualified: Adviser was a competent professional with sufficient expertise
view.s6664.rel.specific_advice: Advice specific to taxpayer's facts (not generic)
view.s6672.def.actual_payments: Made actual payments on tax liability when funds available
view.s6672.def.bona_fide_dispute: Bona fide dispute over employee classification (1099 vs W-2)
view.s6672.def.consider_chapter_11: Chapter 11 bankruptcy can suspend personal collection action
view.s6672.def.early_knowledge: Recently joined + had no opportunity to act before taxes due
view.s6672.def.encumbered: "Funds were encumbered" — bank loan covenants required other creditor payment
view.s6672.def.not_responsible: "Not responsible person" — limited authority + no payment decision power
view.s6672.def.not_willful: "Not willful" — didn't know taxes due + reasonable belief paid + no unencumbered funds
view.s6672.def.partial_designation: Designate partial payments to trust fund vs employer portions
view.s6672.def.s6672_contribution: § 6672(c) seek contribution from other responsible persons
view.s6672.def.unauthorized: Unauthorized actions by junior employees — not personally responsible
view.s6672.h1.title: // § 6672 TRUST FUND RECOVERY PENALTY
view.s6672.hint.intro: 100% personal penalty on responsible person who willfully fails to pay over trust fund taxes (withheld income tax + employee FICA). Joint and several liability + corp veil pierced.
view.s6672.proc.form_2751: Form 2751 — Proposed Assessment of Trust Fund Recovery Penalty
view.s6672.proc.investigation: IRS investigation: Form 4180 interview + financial records
view.s6672.proc.refund: Refund suit: Flora rule — pay 1 quarter + 1 employee's TFRP, sue for refund
view.s6672.proc.s6320_cdp: § 6320 CDP hearing — lien notice within 5 business days
view.s6672.proc.s6321_lien: § 6321 lien — automatic on failure to pay
view.s6672.resp.bookkeeper: Bookkeeper / accountant: typically NOT responsible (no authority to disregard)
view.s6672.resp.check_signing: Check-signing authority (actual exercise, not just title)
view.s6672.resp.multiple_persons: Multiple responsible persons common in same corporation
view.s6672.resp.officer: Corporate officer or director with PRACTICAL AUTHORITY (titles alone not dispositive)
view.s6672.resp.outside_director: Outside director without practical authority: typically NOT responsible
view.s6672.resp.payroll_authority: Authority to direct payroll + creditor payments
view.s6672.resp.signature: Signature authority on corporate bank accounts
view.s6672.resp.tax_filing: Authority over filing tax returns + paying taxes
view.s6672.will.chose: CHOSE to pay other creditors instead
view.s6672.will.encumbered: "Encumbered" funds (i.e., security interest holder claim): NOT available — exception
view.s6672.will.knew_after: Person who LEARNS of unpaid taxes after-the-fact + has UNencumbered funds: willful if doesn't pay
view.s6672.will.no_evil_motive: No "evil motive" required — paying creditors over taxes IS willful
view.s6672.will.partial: Partial payment of trust fund taxes: STILL willful for remainder
view.s6672.will.reckless: Reckless disregard counts as willful (Slodov v. United States)
view.s6694.h1.title: // § 6694 PREPARER PENALTY
view.s6694.hint.intro: (a) Unreasonable position: lesser of $1,000 OR 50% of preparer's fee. (b) Willful or reckless: lesser of $5,000 OR 75% of preparer's fee. Reasonable-basis standard: ~33% chance of being sustained. Tax-shelter / reportable transaction: more-likely-than-not standard (>50%). Disclosure (Form 8275 / Form 8275-R) on aggressive position avoids (a). Required Practitioner Registration (PTIN) annually.
view.s6694.rel.6695a: § 6695(a) Failure to furnish copy of return: $60/failure (max $30k)
view.s6694.rel.6695g: § 6695(g) Failure to comply with EITC due diligence: $635/failure (2024)
view.s6694.rel.6700: § 6700 Abusive tax shelter promoter: 50% of gross income from activity
view.s6694.rel.7216: § 7216 Unauthorized disclosure of taxpayer info: $1k + 1-yr prison
view.s6695.c230.fees: § 10.27 fees: no contingent fees on original returns
view.s6695.dd_note: § 6695(g) due diligence FAILURES. Required: Form 8867 each return + retain 3-year records. Penalty PER CREDIT per return — EITC + CTC + AOTC + HoH on same return = up to $635 × 4 = $2,540 per return. IRS preparer compliance unit (CDX) audits high-volume EITC preparers. Implement systematic checklist + documentation procedures.
view.s6695.gg.cant_solely_rely: Cannot rely solely on taxpayer's word — verify documentation
view.s6695.gg.cumulative: EITC + CTC + AOTC + HoH on same return = up to $635 × 4 = $2,540 per return
view.s6695.gg.documentation: Document: SSN cards, dependent records, qualifying expense receipts
view.s6695.gg.form_8867: Form 8867 (Paid Preparer's Due Diligence Checklist) required EACH RETURN
view.s6695.gg.penalty_per_credit: PENALTY PER CREDIT per return — multiple credits → multiple penalties
view.s6695.gg.questions: Required: ask appropriate questions + verify taxpayer eligibility
view.s6695.gg.records: Retain ALL records 3 years from filing date
view.s6695.gg.scope: Refundable credits: EITC, Child Tax Credit (refundable), American Opportunity Tax Credit, Head of Household status
view.s6695.h1.title: // § 6695 PREPARER PENALTIES
view.s6695.h2.s6695g_diligence: § 6695(g) — EITC + CTC + AOTC + HoH due diligence
view.s6695.hint.intro: Various FLAT PENALTIES for procedural failures by tax return preparers. § 6695(a): failure to furnish copy of return to taxpayer — $60 / return. § 6695(b): failure to sign as preparer — $60. § 6695(c): failure to maintain list of returns prepared — $60. § 6695(g): failure to perform due diligence on EITC, CTC, AOTC, HoH — $635 / failure (2024). Form 8867 required for each refundable credit return. Annual penalty caps by failure type. Reasonable cause exception limited.
view.s6695.rc.appeals: Appeals: petition Tax Court within 30 days of denial
view.s6695.rc.documentation: Document reasonable steps taken to verify
view.s6695.rc.factors: Factors: experience, complexity of return, supervisor approval
view.s6695.rc.first_time: First-time penalty: harder to abate (no automatic abatement)
view.s6695.rc.no_reliance: Cannot rely on taxpayer's representations without verification
view.s6695.rc.reliance: Reliance on substantial authority + adequate disclosure
view.s6695.rc.standard: Limited reasonable cause exception (Boyle case strict)
view.s67.above.s162_se: § 162 Self-employed Schedule C — fully deductible above-line
view.s67.above.s62_a_15: § 62(a)(15) — attorney fees for civil rights / qui tam recoveries
view.s67.h1.title: // § 67 2% MISC ITEMIZED FLOOR + TCJA SUSPENSION
view.s67.h2.not_suspended: § 67(b) deductions NOT subject to 2% floor (still allowed)
view.s67.hint.intro: Pre-TCJA: 2% AGI floor. § 67(g) TCJA SUSPENDS misc itemized 2018-2025. § 67(b) exceptions (gambling/IRD/impairment) + § 67(e) estate/trust above-line preserved.
view.s67.ns.gambling: Gambling losses to extent of winnings (§ 165(d))
view.s67.ns.iif: Estate tax deduction on IRD — § 691(c) (the FULL deduction, not the portion above 2%)
view.s67.ns.s170_charitable: § 170 charitable contributions (subject to AGI %, NOT 2% misc floor)
view.s67.plan.accountable: Accountable plan: substantiate + return excess + business-purpose
view.s67.plan.employer: Employees: get expenses REIMBURSED by employer (accountable plan) — avoids § 67(g)
view.s67.plan.investment_management: Investment management fees: NOT deductible UNTIL § 67(g) sunsets
view.s67.plan.qualified_disaster: Casualty losses: only federally declared disasters deductible
view.s67.plan.s125: Cafeteria plan / DCAP / HSA — exclude expenses from income
view.s67.plan.s162: S-corp: pay business expenses + reimburse via accountable plan
view.s67.plan.s475: Securities traders: § 475(f) MTM election converts § 212 expenses to § 162 (above-line)
view.s67.plan.sunset_2026: Plan for sunset Dec 31, 2025 → 2% floor returns 2026
view.s67.plan.tax_court: Tax court appeals: § 67(g) suspension argument upheld (limited exceptions)
view.s67.plan.work_home_office: Self-employment / S-corp salary mix: convert to deductible above-line
view.s67.s67e.beneficiaries: Excess deductions on termination: pass to beneficiaries (post-2017 fix per § 67(g))
view.s67.s67e.bundled: Bundled fees: portion attributable to investment advice (§ 212) NOT deductible
view.s67.s67e.fiduciary: Fiduciary fees + administration expenses UNIQUE to estate/trust: ABOVE-LINE
view.s67.s67e.knight: Knight v. Comm. (SCOTUS 2008) — investment advisor fees subject to 2% floor
view.s67.s67e.notice_2018_61: Notice 2018-61 — § 67(g) does NOT suspend § 67(e) deductions for estates/trusts
view.s67.s67e.reg_2014: Reg § 1.67-4 (2014) — clarifies post-Knight: bundled fee unbundling required
view.s67.s67e.unique: Test: would an INDIVIDUAL have incurred this expense?
view.s67.susp.appraisal: Appraisal fees (charitable contribution-related: still requires appraisal but fee not deductible)
view.s67.susp.iif_estate: Estate tax deduction allocated to income in respect of decedent (IRD) — § 691(c)
view.s67.susp.legal_taxable: Legal fees in connection with TAXABLE income
view.s67.susp.safe: Safe deposit box rental for taxable securities
view.s6700.h1.title: // § 6700 PROMOTER PENALTY
view.s6700.hint.intro: Penalty on PROMOTERS of abusive tax shelters who: (1) make false/fraudulent statements about tax benefits OR (2) provide GROSS VALUATION OVERSTATEMENTS. Penalty: GREATER of $1,000 per activity OR 100% of gross income derived from the activity. Separate from § 6701 aid + abet ($1,000 individuals; $10,000 corps). Listed Transactions + Reportable Transactions: disclosure requirements + heightened penalties. Forms 8918 + 8886.
view.s6700.listed_note: LISTED TRANSACTION: heightened enforcement. § 6707 $50K failure-to-register + § 6707A $5-200K failure-to-disclose. Material advisor list § 6112 must be maintained + provided to IRS within 20 days. Statute of limitations re-opens 1 yr after disclosure required. DOJ injunction also available (§ 7408).
view.s6700.lt.crispr: CRISPR / R&D credit abuse arrangements — recent
view.s6700.lt.son_of_boss: Son of BOSS / similar — Notice 99-59 (older but active enforcement)
view.s6700.rep.crossborder: FATCA + CRS reporting overlap for offshore arrangements
view.s6700.rep.failure_disclosure: Failure to disclose: $50,000 per failure (more for listed)
view.s6700.rep.form_8886: Form 8886 — Reportable Transaction disclosure by taxpayer
view.s6700.rep.list: Maintained list of advisees (Material Advisor List § 6112)
view.s6700.rep.list_30day: Provide list to IRS within 20 days of request
view.s6700.rep.reportable_transaction_ty: 5 reportable transaction categories: listed, confidential, contractual, loss, transaction of interest
view.s6700.rep.statute_listed: Listed transactions: statute of limitations re-opens for 1 yr after disclosure required
view.s6700.scope.advisor: Material advisor (§ 6111): provides tax-related material aid
view.s6700.scope.compensation_link: Penalty applies to person who derives INCOME from promotion
view.s6700.scope.knowledge_or_should: 'Knew or had reason to know' of false statement
view.s6700.scope.promoter: Promoter: organizes / participates in sale of plan / arrangement
view.s6700.scope.seller: Seller: who sells interests in the arrangement
view.s6700.scope.tax_benefits: 'Tax benefits' includes deductions, credits, exclusions, deferral
view.s6707a.h1.title: // § 6707A REPORTABLE TRANSACTION
view.s6707a.hint.intro: 5 categories: Listed (most aggressive) / Confidential / Loss / Transaction-of-Interest / Contractual Protection. Penalty: 75% of decrease in tax claimed (min $10k / $50k entity, max $100k / $200k entity). Reportable on Form 8886. Strict liability — no reasonable cause defense.
view.s6707a.label.name: Transaction name
view.s6707a.material.body: Material Advisors who promote / draft / sell Reportable Transactions face their own penalties: § 6707: $50k flat (or $200k for Listed) for failing to file Form 8918. § 6708: $10k per day (max $50k) for not maintaining investor / advisee list. Form 13976 required to disclose advisor identity to investors.
view.s6707a.stack.7203: Criminal § 7203 willful failure to file (1-yr misdemeanor)
view.s6707a.th.name: Name
view.s6724.de_minimis.body: 10 returns OR 0.5% of total, whichever is less, with INCORRECT information are PENALTY-FREE if cured by August 1. Excellent reason to do error-correcting Form W-2c / 1099-CORR by July 31. Doesn't apply to 'intentional disregard' failures.
view.s6724.h1.title: // § 6721/22/24 INFO RETURN PENALTIES
view.s6724.hint.intro: § 6721 Failure to file + § 6722 Failure to furnish payee stack. Penalties: $60/$130/$310 per return by tier (cured within 30 days / by Aug 1 / after). $630 intentional. Annual caps: $1.26M small biz / $3.78M large. § 6724 reasonable cause waiver + De Minimis safe harbor (10 or 0.5% of forms cured by Aug 1, whichever less).
view.s6724.rc.acted_quickly: Acted quickly to correct upon discovery
view.s6724.rc.events_beyond_control: Events beyond filer's control (records destroyed, key employee disabled, etc.)
view.s6724.rc.no_warning: No advance IRS warning of the issue
view.s6724.rc.significant_mitigating: Significant mitigating factors despite normal business care
view.s6724.rc.system_software: Software / system failure beyond filer's control
view.s6724.rel.6651: § 6651 Failure to file / pay TAX returns (separate from info returns)
view.s6724.rel.6672: § 6672 Trust Fund Recovery Penalty (TFRP): 100% on unpaid trust fund taxes
view.s6724.rel.6722: § 6722 Failure to furnish CORRECT payee statement to recipient
view.s6724.rel.6723: § 6723 Failure to comply with other info reporting ($60/failure)
view.s6724.rel.electronic: Electronic filing requirement (≥ 10 returns 2024): $310/failure if paper instead
view.s6724.rel.intentional: Intentional disregard penalty: $630/return or 10% of amount required
view.s6724.rel.payee_w9: Failure to obtain W-9 backup withholding $310/failure
view.s691.h1.title: // § 691 IRD INCOME IN RESPECT OF DECEDENT
view.s691.hint.intro: Income earned but not received before death. Beneficiary reports as ORDINARY income when received. NO § 1014 step-up in basis. § 691(c) deduction: recipient takes income tax deduction for estate tax attributable to IRD — prevents double taxation. Most common: traditional IRA / 401(k) balances, accrued interest, accounts receivable, NQ deferred comp, deferred IRC § 453 installment sale.
view.s691.plan.691c: § 691(c) deduction is a MISC itemized — suspended by TCJA through 2025?
view.s691.plan.charity: Name charity as IRA beneficiary — charity avoids income tax
view.s691.plan.crt: Charitable Remainder Trust as IRA beneficiary — stretches income
view.s691.plan.nimcrut: NIMCRUT funded with IRA for stretch-like outcome
view.s691.plan.qpb: 'Eligible designated beneficiary' exceptions: spouse, minor child, disabled, < 10 yr younger
view.s691.plan.roth_convert: Pre-death Roth conversion: removes IRD treatment, gets future-tax-free
view.s691.plan.stretch_secure: SECURE Act 2020 killed lifetime stretch — 10-yr cap for non-spouse
view.s707.a.purpose: Partner transacts with partnership AS IF NOT a partner
view.s707.a.related_party: Related party transactions: arm's length scrutiny + § 482-like
view.s707.a.s707_a_2_a: § 707(a)(2)(A) — distinction between bona fide allocation/distribution + § 707(a) transaction
view.s707.a.services: Services: compensation income to partner, deduction by PS
view.s707.b.brother_sister: "Brother-sister" entities: both controlled by same partner = § 707(b)(2) applies
view.s707.b.disallow_carryover: Disallowed loss: lost to seller; basis NOT increased to buyer either
view.s707.b.gain: § 707(b)(2) — GAIN on sale between commonly-controlled PSs: ORDINARY (recharacterization)
view.s707.b.loss: § 707(b)(1) — LOSS on sale partner ↔ controlled PS: DISALLOWED entirely
view.s707.b.s267: Compare § 267: similar disallowance for related individuals + entities
view.s707.b.s267_d: § 267(d) subsequent gain: buyer offsets prior disallowed loss when buyer sells
view.s707.c.documentation: Documentation in partnership agreement key for characterization
view.s707.c.fixed: Fixed payment determined WITHOUT regard to partnership income
view.s707.c.like_compensation: Treated as § 162 compensation (deductible by PS, ordinary to partner)
view.s707.c.no_partner_status: Does NOT confer partner status if not otherwise partner
view.s707.c.preferred_return: Preferred return on capital: may be guaranteed payment OR § 704(b) allocation
view.s707.c.priority_distribution: Distinguish from PRIORITY DISTRIBUTION (varies with PS income) — § 704(b) allocation
view.s707.c.s162_ordinary: PS deducts as § 162 ordinary expense unless capital expenditure
view.s707.c.se_tax: Subject to SE tax (§ 1402(a)(13) general partner exception does NOT apply)
view.s707.c.timing: Timing: deducted by PS when accrued/paid (Rev. Rul. 2007-40)
view.s707.dis.2year: 2-YEAR presumption: contribution + distribution within 2 years = sale
view.s707.dis.assumed_liability: Assumed liability counted as part of "distribution" (Reg § 1.707-5)
view.s707.dis.factors: Factors: timing, conditioning, certainty, entrepreneurial risk, related parties

view.s707.h1.title: // § 707 PARTNER-PARTNERSHIP TRANSACTIONS
view.s707.hint.intro: § 707(a)(2)(B) disguised sale 2-yr presumption. § 707(b) related party loss disallowance + gain ordinary. § 707(c) guaranteed payments + SE tax.
view.s707.safe.cash_flow: Cash flow distributions: based on partnership income — safe
view.s707.safe.operating_dist: Operating distributions: not deemed sale if pursuant to bona fide PS agreement
view.s707.safe.preferred_return: Preferred return: safe harbor for 150% of AFR — not deemed disguised sale
view.s707.safe.qualified_liab: Qualified liabilities (Reg § 1.707-5(a)(6)) — not counted as distribution
view.s707.safe.reimbursement: Reimbursement of preformation costs: within 2 years OK if < 20% FMV at contribution
view.s707.safe.s704_c_7yr: § 704(c)(1)(B) 7-year built-in gain recognition on contributed property
view.s707.safe.s737_seven_year: § 737 7-yr lookback parallel for built-in gain protection
view.s7122.ale.food_clothing: Food + clothing + personal care + household supplies + miscellaneous
view.s7122.ale.transport: Transportation: ownership + operating costs by region
view.s7122.h1.title: // § 7122 OFFER IN COMPROMISE
view.s7122.hint.intro: IRS settles for less when full collection doubtful. 3 grounds: (1) Doubt as to Collectibility (DATC), (2) Doubt as to Liability (DATL), (3) Effective Tax Administration (ETA). Reasonable Collection Potential (RCP) = net equity + future income × 12 mo (lump) or 24 mo (periodic). $205 application fee + Form 656 + Form 433-A(OIC) / 433-B(OIC). Low-income waiver available.
view.s7122.po.application_fee: $205 application fee (waived if low-income or DATL)
view.s7122.po.default: Default: full balance owed + add'l penalties
view.s7122.po.deferred: Deferred Payment Plan: pay over remaining CSED (rare, after IA failure)
view.s7122.po.during_eval: Payments during eval keep accruing — non-refundable if rejected
view.s7122.po.lump_sum: Lump sum: 20% with offer + 5 payments within 5 months of acceptance
view.s7122.po.periodic: Periodic: initial + monthly payments DURING evaluation + after acceptance, 24 mo total
view.s72p.ben.flexible: Can be re-paid early without penalty
view.s72p.ben.interest_to_you: Interest goes BACK to your account (paying yourself)
view.s72p.ben.low_rate: Interest typically lower than credit cards / personal loans
view.s72p.ben.no_credit: No credit check (loan from your own balance)
view.s72p.ben.no_tax: Not a taxable distribution if repaid on time
view.s72p.h1.title: // § 72(p) 401(k) PLAN LOAN
view.s72p.hint.intro: Max: lesser of $50,000 OR 50% vested balance. 5-year max term (up to 30 yrs for principal residence). Quarterly payments minimum. Loan default = deemed distribution + 10% penalty if < 59½. SECURE 2.0: Roth source for loans + plan-to-plan rollover loans preserved. Hidden cost: opportunity cost (money out of market) + double taxation of interest (you repay with after-tax dollars, then pay tax again on withdrawal).
view.s72p.risk.balloon: Balloon payment at term-end if quarterly amounts insufficient
view.s72p.risk.contributions: Suspend new contributions during loan period (some plans)
view.s72p.risk.double_tax: Double-taxation: interest paid with after-tax dollars + taxed again on withdrawal
view.s72p.risk.job_loss: Job loss / separation triggers loan acceleration (60-90 days to repay)
view.s72p.risk.market_timing: Borrowing in down market locks in losses
view.s72p.risk.opportunity: Opportunity cost: money OUT of market while loan outstanding
view.s72t.h1.title: // § 72(t) SEPP CALCULATOR
view.s72t.hint.intro: Early IRA / 401(k) withdrawal before 59½ without 10% penalty via Substantially Equal Periodic Payments. THREE approved methods: RMD, Amortization, Annuitization. Must continue ≥ 5 years OR until 59½ (whichever later). MODIFY in those years and 10% penalty applies back to year 1 + interest. SECURE 2.0 floors rate at max(5%, 120% AFR).
view.s72t.mod.account_zero: Account goes to zero (no longer 'substantially equal')
view.s72t.mod.amount_change: Changing the amount (except amort→RMD one-time switch)
view.s72t.mod.rollover: Rolling money IN OR OUT of the SEPP account
view.s72t.note.amort: Balance amortized at rate over life expectancy
view.s731.basic.gain: § 731(a)(1) — gain only to extent cash > outside basis
view.s731.basic.loss: § 731(a)(2) — loss only on LIQUIDATION + cash + receivables + inventory distributed
view.s731.basic.no_intermediate: No "intermediate" gain on appreciated non-cash property distributions
view.s731.basic.s731c_reduction: § 731(c)(3) reduction: pro-rata share of partnership's gain in securities
view.s731.h1.title: // § 731 PARTNERSHIP DISTRIBUTIONS
view.s731.hint.intro: Gain if cash distributed > outside basis. Loss only on liquidating distribution of cash + receivables + inventory. § 731(c) marketable securities treated as money.
view.s731.s731c.actively_traded: "Actively traded" = OTC market or established exchange
view.s731.s731c.basis_property: Partner's basis in distributed securities: § 732 (cash treatment)
view.s731.s731c.defined: Securities = stock, bonds, options, futures, foreign currency, derivatives
view.s731.s731c.fmv: Distributed at FMV — treated as MONEY for § 731(a)(1)
view.s731.s731c.formula: Reduction = (partner's distributive share) × (PS unrealized gain in security)
view.s731.s731c.reduction: § 731(c)(3) reduces FMV by partner's share of unrealized gain
view.s731.s731c.s721_contributed: Securities CONTRIBUTED by partner: exempt from § 731(c) treatment
view.s731.s731c.s721_purchased: Securities PURCHASED by partnership: subject to § 731(c)
view.s731.s737.basis_inc: Basis in distributed property: § 732 — but PLUS § 737(d) gain recognized
view.s731.s737.character: Character: same as contributed property would have on partnership sale
view.s731.s737.formula: Gain = LESSER of (precontribution gain) OR (excess of distributed FMV over outside basis)
view.s731.s737.partner_only: Only contributing partner — not other partners
view.s731.s737.purpose: Anti-abuse: contributing partner recognizes precontribution gain if other property distributed within 7 years
view.s731.s737.s704c_relation: Coordinates with § 704(c)(1)(B) similar gain recognition rule
view.s731.s737.s721_exception: § 721 contributions not triggering: gift, like-kind, charitable, etc.
view.s731.s751b.aggregate_basis: Partnership aggregate basis in hot assets adjusted accordingly
view.s731.s751b.capital_gain: Capital gain to recipient on cold assets received in excess
view.s731.s751b.character: Ordinary income to recipient on hot assets received in excess
view.s731.s751b.deemed_sale: Deemed sale at FMV of disproportionate amount
view.s731.s751b.disproportionate: If partner receives MORE / LESS hot assets than pro-rata share
view.s731.s751b.exchange: Treated as TAXABLE EXCHANGE between partner + partnership
view.s731.s751b.hot_assets: "Hot assets" = unrealized receivables + substantially appreciated inventory
view.s731.s751b.s751a: § 751(a) different: applies to sale of partnership interest (always)
view.s731.tbl.equal_basis: Partner outside basis allocated among property
view.s731.tbl.lesser: Lesser of (P/S basis in property) OR (partner outside basis)
view.s731.tbl.r_basis: Carryover basis (or lower of basis / FMV)

view.s7345.excl.identity: Identified by IRS as identity theft victim
view.s7345.h1.title: // § 7345 PASSPORT REVOCATION
view.s7345.hint.intro: 'Seriously delinquent tax debt' = > $62,000 (2024, indexed) assessed federal tax + lien filed OR levy issued. IRS certifies to State Dept; passport revoked / denied. CP508C notice issued; right to reverse via payment / installment agreement / CDP hearing. Issued passports may be LIMITED to direct US return. Foreign / US-Mexican border crossings denied.
view.s7345.rem.cdp_request: Request CDP hearing (Form 12153 within 30 days of notice)
view.s7345.rem.court: § 7345(e) suit in district court / Tax Court (limited remedies)
view.s7345.rem.emergency: Emergency passport for direct US return: contact embassy/consulate
view.s7345.rem.expedite: Imminent international travel: contact Taxpayer Advocate for expedited reversal
view.s7345.rem.pay_full: Pay in full — IRS reverses within 30 days
view.s7345.warning.act_now: ACT NOW: enter installment agreement / OIC / CDP hearing BEFORE IRS certifies to State Dept. Once certified (CP508C), passport revocation can be immediate on next application / renewal. 30-day reversal after qualifying action.

view.s736.a.guaranteed: If fixed amount → § 707(c) guaranteed payment (PS deducts ordinary)

view.s736.a.s451: § 451 timing — accrual at fixed/determinable + economic performance
view.s736.a.se_tax: Subject to SE tax (if partner was general partner)
view.s736.a.spread_dedn: PS gets ordinary deduction spread across payment periods
view.s736.alloc.cap_intensive_default: Capital partnership: all § 736(b) by default — limited flexibility
view.s736.alloc.deal_term: Deal term: allocation in retirement agreement

view.s736.alloc.optimization: Optimize: balance partner's character + PS deduction

view.s736.alloc.partner_low_bracket: Partner low tax bracket: prefer § 736(a) (ordinary at low rate + PS deducts)

view.s736.b.no_ps_deduction: PS does NOT deduct § 736(b) payments — capital character

view.s736.b2.s197: § 197 intangibles — generally retiring partner's share of acquired goodwill

view.s736.h1.title: // § 736 RETIRING / DECEASED PARTNER PAYMENTS
view.s736.hint.intro: § 736(b) capital for partnership property. § 736(a) ordinary (distributive share or guaranteed payment). § 736(b)(2) service PS exception for unrealized receivables + unstated goodwill.

view.s743.hint.intro: § 743(b) aligns transferee inside basis with outside basis when § 754 election OR substantial built-in loss > $250K. § 755 allocates between property classes.

view.s743.rel.s731: § 731 — recognition of gain on distribution exceeding outside basis
view.s743.rel.s732: § 732 — basis of distributed property to recipient partner
view.s743.rel.s732_e: § 732(e) — mandatory basis reduction for built-in loss property distribution

view.s743.rel.s7704: § 7704 PTP — special rules for publicly traded partnerships

view.s743.s754.k1: Form K-1 must reflect partner's § 743(b) adjustment annually
